text.NRSV: The words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah, of the priests who were in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin,
text.Rotherham: The words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah,—of the priests who were in Anathoth, in the land of Benjamin:
text.KJV: The words of Jeremiah the son of Hilkiah, of the priests that were in Anathoth in the land of Benjamin:
text.NABRE: I. Oracles in the Days of Josiah Chapter 1 The words of Jeremiah, son of Hilkiah, one of the priests from Anathoth, in the land of Benjamin.
text.DRB: The words of Jeremy the son of Helcias, of the Priests that were in Anathoth, in the land of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: to whom the word of the Lord came in the days of King Josiah son of Amon of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.
text.Rotherham: unto whom came the word of Yahweh, in the days of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah,—in the thirteenth year of his reign;
text.KJV: To whom the word of the Lord came in the days of Josiah the son of Amon king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign.
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to him in the days of Josiah, son of Amon, king of Judah, in the thirteenth year of his reign,
text.DRB: The word of our Lord which was made to him in the days of Josias the son of Amon king of Juda, in the thirteenth year of his kingdom.

text.NRSV: It came also in the days of King Jehoiakim son of Josiah of Judah, and until the end of the eleventh year of King Zedekiah son of Josiah of Judah, until the captivity of Jerusalem in the fifth month.
text.Rotherham: it came also in the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah, king of Judah, unto the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah son of Josiah king of Judah,—as far as the carrying away of Jerusalem captive, in the fifth month.
text.KJV: It came also in the days of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah the son of Josiah king of Judah, unto the carrying away of Jerusalem captive in the fifth month.
text.NABRE: and again in the days of Jehoiakim, son of Josiah, king of Judah, until the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah, son of Josiah, king of Judah—down to the exile of Jerusalem, in the fifth month.
text.DRB: And the word was made in the days of Joakim the son of Josias king of Juda, unto the end of the eleventh year of Sedecias the son of Josias king of Juda, even unto the transmigration of Jerusalem, in the fifth month.

text.NRSV: Now the word of the Lord came to me saying,
text.Rotherham: So then, the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying:
text.KJV: Then the word of the Lord came unto me, saying,
text.NABRE: Call of Jeremiah The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, and before you were born I consecrated you; I appointed you a prophet to the nations.”
text.Rotherham: Before I formed thee at thy birth, I took knowledge of thee, And before thy nativity, I hallowed thee,—A prophet to the nations, I appointed thee.
text.KJV: Before I formed thee in the belly I knew thee; and before thou camest forth out of the womb I sanctified thee, and I ordained thee a prophet unto the nations.
text.NABRE: Before I formed you in the womb I knew you, before you were born I dedicated you, a prophet to the nations I appointed you.
text.DRB: Before I formed thee in the womb, I knew thee: and before thou camest forth of the matrice, I sanctified thee, and a Prophet in the Gentiles I gave thee.

text.NRSV: Then I said, “Ah, Lord God! Truly I do not know how to speak, for I am only a boy.”
text.Rotherham: Then said I—Ah! My Lord Yahweh! Lo! I know not how to speak,—For a child, am I!
text.KJV: Then said I, Ah, Lord God! behold, I cannot speak: for I am a child.
text.NABRE: “Ah, Lord God!” I said, “I do not know how to speak. I am too young!”
text.DRB: And I said: A a a, o Lord God: Behold I cannot speak, because I am a child.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to me, “Do not say, ‘I am only a boy’; for you shall go to all to whom I send you, and you shall speak whatever I command you.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me, Do not say, A child, am, I,—For against whomsoever I send thee, shalt thou go, And whatsoever I command thee, shalt thou speak:
text.KJV: But the Lord said unto me, Say not, I am a child: for thou shalt go to all that I shall send thee, and whatsoever I command thee thou shalt speak.
text.NABRE: But the Lord answered me, Do not say, “I am too young.” To whomever I send you, you shall go; whatever I command you, you shall speak.
text.DRB: And our Lord said unto me: Say not: I am a child: for to all things, to which I shall send thee, thou shalt go: and all things whatsoever I shall command thee, thou shalt speak.

text.NRSV: Do not be afraid of them, for I am with you to deliver you, says the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Be not afraid of their faces, For with thee, am I, to deliver thee Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Be not afraid of their faces: for I am with thee to deliver thee, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Do not be afraid of them, for I am with you to deliver you—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Be not afraid of their face: because I am with thee, to deliver thee, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord put out his hand and touched my mouth; and the Lord said to me, “Now I have put my words in your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh put forth his hand, and touched my mouth,—and Yahweh said unto me, Lo! I have put my words in thy mouth.
text.KJV: Then the Lord put forth his hand, and touched my mouth. And the Lord said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord extended his hand and touched my mouth, saying to me, See, I place my words in your mouth!
text.DRB: And our Lord put forth his hand, and touched my mouth: & our Lord said to me: Behold I have given my words in thy mouth:

text.NRSV: See, today I appoint you over nations and over kingdoms, to pluck up and to pull down, to destroy and to overthrow, to build and to plant.”
text.Rotherham: See! I have set thee in charge this day, over the nations and over the kingdoms, To uproot and to break down, and to destroy and to tear in pieces,—To build and to plant.
text.KJV: See, I have this day set thee over the nations and over the kingdoms, to root out, and to pull down, and to destroy, and to throw down, to build, and to plant.
text.NABRE: Today I appoint you over nations and over kingdoms, To uproot and to tear down, to destroy and to demolish, to build and to plant.
text.DRB: Behold I have appointed thee this day over the Gentiles, and over kingdoms, that thou mayst pluck up, and destroy, and waste, and dissipate, and build, and plant.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me, saying, “Jeremiah, what do you see?” And I said, “I see a branch of an almond tree.”
text.Rotherham: Moreover the Word of Yahweh came unto me saying, What canst thou see Jeremiah? And I said, A twig of an almond-tree, can I see.
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came unto me, saying, Jeremiah, what seest thou? And I said, I see a rod of an almond tree.
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to me: What do you see, Jeremiah? “I see a branch of the almond tree,” I replied.
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying: What seest thou Jeremy? And I said: I see a rod watching.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to me, “You have seen well, for I am watching over my word to perform it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me—Thou hast rightly seen,—for keeping watch, am I over my word to perform it.
text.KJV: Then said the Lord unto me, Thou hast well seen: for I will hasten my word to perform it.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to me: You have seen well, for I am watching over my word to carry it out.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: Well hast thou seen; because I will watch upon my word to do it.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me a second time, saying, “What do you see?” And I said, “I see a boiling pot, tilting away from the north.”
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me a second time saying, What canst thou see? And I said, A boiling caldron, can I see, with, the front thereof, on the North.
text.KJV: And the word of the Lord came unto me the second time, saying, What seest thou? And I said, I see a seething pot; and the face thereof is toward the north.
text.NABRE: A second time the word of the Lord came to me: What do you see? I replied, “I see a boiling kettle whose mouth is tipped away from the north.”
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me the second time, saying: What seest thou? And I said: I see a pot boiling hot, and the face thereof from the face of the North.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to me: Out of the north disaster shall break out on all the inhabitants of the land.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me,—Out of the North, shall break forth calamity, against all the inhabitants of the land.
text.KJV: Then the Lord said unto me, Out of the north an evil shall break forth upon all the inhabitants of the land.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to me, And from the north evil will pour out over all who dwell in the land.
text.DRB: And our Lord said unto me: From the North shall evil be opened upon all the inhabitants of the land.

text.NRSV: For now I am calling all the tribes of the kingdoms of the north, says the Lord; and they shall come and all of them shall set their thrones at the entrance of the gates of Jerusalem, against all its surrounding walls and against all the cities of Judah.
text.Rotherham: For behold me! calling for all the families of the kingdoms of the North, Declareth Yahweh,—and they shall come, and set every one his throne at the opening of the gates of Jerusalem and against all her walls round about, and against all the cities of Judah!
text.KJV: For, lo, I will call all the families of the kingdoms of the north, saith the Lord; and they shall come, and they shall set every one his throne at the entering of the gates of Jerusalem, and against all the walls thereof round about, and against all the cities of Judah.
text.NABRE: Look, I am summoning all the kingdoms of the north—oracle of the Lord — Each king shall come and set up his throne in the gateways of Jerusalem, Against all its surrounding walls and against all the cities of Judah.
text.DRB: Because lo I will call together all the kindreds of the kingdoms of the North, saith our Lord: and they shall come, and shall set every one his throne in the entering of the gates of Jerusalem, and upon all the walls thereof round about, and upon all the cities of Juda.

text.NRSV: And I will utter my judgments against them, for all their wickedness in forsaking me; they have made offerings to other gods, and worshiped the works of their own hands.
text.Rotherham: Then will I pronounce my judgments against them, concerning all their wickedness,—in that they have forsaken me and have burned incense unto other gods, and have bowed down to the works of their own hands,
text.KJV: And I will utter my judgments against them touching all their wickedness, who have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto other gods, and worshipped the works of their own hands.
text.NABRE: I will pronounce my sentence against them for all their wickedness in forsaking me, In burning incense to other gods, in bowing down to the works of their hands.
text.DRB: And I will speak my judgements with them, touching all the wickedness of them that have forsaken me, and have offered to strange gods, and have adored the work of their own hands.

text.NRSV: But you, gird up your loins; stand up and tell them everything that I command you. Do not be dismayed by them, or I will dismay you before them.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, shalt gird thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them, all that, I, command thee,—be not dismayed because of them, lest I dismay thee before their face.
text.KJV: Thou therefore gird up thy loins, and arise, and speak unto them all that I command thee: be not dismayed at their faces, lest I confound thee before them.
text.NABRE: But you, prepare yourself; stand up and tell them all that I command you. Do not be terrified on account of them, or I will terrify you before them;
text.DRB: Thou therefore gird thy loins, & rise, and speak to them all things that I command thee. Be not afraid of their face: for I will make thee not to fear their countenance.

text.NRSV: And I for my part have made you today a fortified city, an iron pillar, and a bronze wall, against the whole land—against the kings of Judah, its princes, its priests, and the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: I, therefore—lo! I have set thee to-day as a fortified city, and as a pillar of iron and as walls of bronze, over all the land,—against the kings of Judah, against her princes, against her priests and against the people of the land.
text.KJV: For, behold, I have made thee this day a defenced city, and an iron pillar, and brasen walls against the whole land, against the kings of Judah, against the princes thereof, against the priests thereof, and against the people of the land.
text.NABRE: For I am the one who today makes you a fortified city, A pillar of iron, a wall of bronze, against the whole land: Against Judah’s kings and princes, its priests and the people of the land.
text.DRB: For I have given thee this day to be as a fenced city, and as an iron pillar, and as a brazen wall over all the land of the kings of Juda, to the Princes thereof, & to the Priests, and to the people of the land.

text.NRSV: They will fight against you; but they shall not prevail against you, for I am with you, says the Lord, to deliver you.”
text.Rotherham: And they will fight against thee but shall not prevail against thee,—for with thee, am I, Declareth Yahweh, to deliver thee.
text.KJV: And they shall fight against thee; but they shall not prevail against thee; for I am with thee, saith the Lord, to deliver thee.
text.NABRE: They will fight against you, but not prevail over you, for I am with you to deliver you—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And they shall fight against thee, and shall not prevail: because I am with thee, saith our Lord, to deliver thee.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to me, saying:
text.Rotherham: And the word of Yahweh came unto me, saying;
text.KJV: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Infidelity of Israel. The word of the Lord came to me:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to me, saying:

text.NRSV: Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem, Thus says the Lord: I remember the devotion of your youth, your love as a bride, how you followed me in the wilderness, in a land not sown.
text.Rotherham: Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem saying—Thus saith Yahweh, I remember in thy behalf, the lovingkindness of thy youthful days, The love of thy bridal estate,—Thy coming out after me, Through the desert, Through a land, not sown:
text.KJV: Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith the Lord; I remember thee, the kindness of thy youth, the love of thine espousals, when thou wentest after me in the wilderness, in a land that was not sown.
text.NABRE: Go, cry out this message for Jerusalem to hear! I remember the devotion of your youth, how you loved me as a bride, Following me in the wilderness, in a land unsown.
text.DRB: Go, and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying: Thus saith our Lord: I have remembered thee, pitying thy youth, and the charity of thy despousing, when thou didst follow me in the desert, in a land that is not sown.

text.NRSV: Israel was holy to the Lord, the first fruits of his harvest. All who ate of it were held guilty; disaster came upon them, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Holiness, was Israel unto Yahweh, The first-fruit of his increase,—All that devoured it, were held guilty, Calamity used to come upon them,—Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Israel was holiness unto the Lord, and the firstfruits of his increase: all that devour him shall offend; evil shall come upon them, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Israel was dedicated to the Lord, the first fruits of his harvest; All who ate of it were held guilty, evil befell them—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Israel is holy to our Lord, the first fruits of his fruits: all they that do devour it do sin: evil shall come upon them, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Hear the word of the Lord, O house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Hear ye the word of Yahweh O house of Jacob, And all the families of the house of Israel.
text.KJV: Hear ye the word of the Lord, O house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel:
text.NABRE: Listen to the word of the Lord, house of Jacob! All you clans of the house of Israel,
text.DRB: Hear ye the word of our Lord, o house of Jacob, and all ye kindreds of the house of Israel:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: What wrong did your ancestors find in me that they went far from me, and went after worthless things, and became worthless themselves?
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh,—What did your fathers find in me by way of perversity, that they removed far from me,—and went after vanity, and became vain?
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, What iniquity have your fathers found in me, that they are gone far from me, and have walked after vanity, and are become vain?
text.NABRE: thus says the Lord: What fault did your ancestors find in me that they withdrew from me, Went after emptiness, and became empty themselves?
text.DRB: thus saith our Lord: What iniquity have your fathers found in me, that they have made themselves far from me, and have walked after vanity, and are become vain?

text.NRSV: They did not say, “Where is the Lord who brought us up from the land of Egypt, who led us in the wilderness, in a land of deserts and pits, in a land of drought and deep darkness, in a land that no one passes through, where no one lives?”
text.Rotherham: Neither said they, Where is Yahweh, Who brought us up out of the land of Egypt,—Who led us Through the desert, Through a land of wastes and clefts, Through a land of parched places and of death-shade, Through a land, Along which no man had passed, And in which no son of earth dwelt?
text.KJV: Neither said they, Where is the Lord that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, that led us through the wilderness, through a land of deserts and of pits, through a land of drought, and of the shadow of death, through a land that no man passed through, and where no man dwelt?
text.NABRE: They did not ask, “Where is the Lord who brought us up from the land of Egypt, Who led us through the wilderness, through a land of wastes and ravines, A land of drought and darkness, a land which no one crosses, where no one dwells?”
text.DRB: And they have not said: Where is our Lord, that made us come up out of the land of Ægypt, that led us through the desert, through a land inhabitable and without way, through a land of thirst, & the image of death, through a land, wherein no man walked, nor any man dwelt?

text.NRSV: I brought you into a plentiful land to eat its fruits and its good things. But when you entered you defiled my land, and made my heritage an abomination.
text.Rotherham: Then brought I you into a country of garden land, to eat the fruit thereof and the goodness thereof,—and yet when ye entered, then defiled ye my land, and mine inheritance, ye made an abomination
text.KJV: And I brought you into a plentiful country, to eat the fruit thereof and the goodness thereof; but when ye entered, ye defiled my land, and made mine heritage an abomination.
text.NABRE: I brought you into the garden land to eat its fine fruits, But you entered and defiled my land, you turned my heritage into an abomination.
text.DRB: And I brought you into the land of Carmel, that you might eat the fruit thereof, and the best things thereof: and being entered in, you have contaminated my land, & made mine inheritance an abomination.

text.NRSV: The priests did not say, “Where is the Lord?” Those who handle the law did not know me; the rulers transgressed against me; the prophets prophesied by Baal, and went after things that do not profit.
text.Rotherham: The priests, said not Where is Yahweh? And, they who handled the law, did not acknowledge me, And the shepherds transgressed against me,—And the prophets, prophesied by Baal, And so after things that could not profit, did they walk.
text.KJV: The priests said not, Where is the Lord? and they that handle the law knew me not: the pastors also transgressed against me, and the prophets prophesied by Baal, and walked after things that do not profit.
text.NABRE: The priests did not ask, “Where is the Lord?” The experts in the law did not know me: the shepherds rebelled against me. The prophets prophesied by Baal, and went after useless idols.
text.DRB: The Priests have not said: Where is our Lord? And they that held the law knew me not: and the Pastors have transgressed against me, and the Prophets have prophesied in Baal, and have followed idols.

text.NRSV: Therefore once more I accuse you, says the Lord, and I accuse your children’s children.
text.Rotherham: Therefore yet further will I plead with you Declareth Yahweh, Yea with your children’s children, will I plead.
text.KJV: Wherefore I will yet plead with you, saith the Lord, and with your children’s children will I plead.
text.NABRE: Therefore I will again accuse you—oracle of the Lord — even your children’s children I will accuse.
text.DRB: Therefore will I yet contend in judgement with you, saith our Lord, and I will plead with your children.

text.NRSV: Cross to the coasts of Cyprus and look, send to Kedar and examine with care; see if there has ever been such a thing.
text.Rotherham: For—pass through the coastlands of the West and see, And unto Kedar, send ye and consider diligently,—And see—whether there hath been the like of this!
text.KJV: For pass over the isles of Chittim, and see; and send unto Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there be such a thing.
text.NABRE: Cross to the coast of Cyprus and see, send to Kedar and carefully inquire: Where has anything like this been done?
text.DRB: Pass ye to the isles of Cethim, and see: and send into Cedar, and consider earnestly, & see if there hath the like thing been done.

text.NRSV: Has a nation changed its gods, even though they are no gods? But my people have changed their glory for something that does not profit.
text.Rotherham: Hath a nation, changed gods, although they were No-gods? Yet my people have changed my glory for that which could not profit!
text.KJV: Hath a nation changed their gods, which are yet no gods? but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.
text.NABRE: Does any other nation change its gods?— even though they are not gods at all! But my people have changed their glory for useless things.
text.DRB: If a nation hath changed their gods, & surely they are not gods: but my people hath changed their glory into an idol.

text.NRSV: Be appalled, O heavens, at this, be shocked, be utterly desolate, says the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Be amazed, O ye heavens, at this,—And shudder, be dried up utterly, Urgeth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Be astonished, O ye heavens, at this, and be horribly afraid, be ye very desolate, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Be horrified at this, heavens; shudder, be appalled—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Be astonished, o heavens, upon this, and o gates thereof be ye desolate exceedingly, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: for my people have committed two evils: they have forsaken me, the fountain of living water, and dug out cisterns for themselves, cracked cisterns that can hold no water.
text.Rotherham: For two wicked things, have my people committed,—Me, have they forsaken a fountain of living water, To hew out for themselves cisterns broken cisterns, that cannot hold water.
text.KJV: For my people have committed two evils; they have forsaken me the fountain of living waters, and hewed them out cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.
text.NABRE: Two evils my people have done: they have forsaken me, the source of living waters; They have dug themselves cisterns, broken cisterns that cannot hold water.
text.DRB: For two evils hath my people done, Me they have forsaken the fountain of living water, & have digged to themselves cisterns: broken cisterns, that are not able to hold waters.

text.NRSV: Is Israel a slave? Is he a home-born servant? Why then has he become plunder?
text.Rotherham: Was Israel a servant? Born in the house, was he? Wherefore hath he become a prey?
text.KJV: Is Israel a servant? is he a homeborn slave? why is he spoiled?
text.NABRE: Is Israel a slave, a house-born servant? Why then has he become plunder?
text.DRB: Why, is Israel a bondman, or a servant born in the house? Why then is he become a prey?

text.NRSV: The lions have roared against him, they have roared loudly. They have made his land a waste; his cities are in ruins, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: Against him, have been roaring wild lions, They have uttered their voice,—and have made his land a desolation, His cities, have been burned, so as to have no inhabitant.
text.KJV: The young lions roared upon him, and yelled, and they made his land waste: his cities are burned without inhabitant.
text.NABRE: Against him lions roar, they raise their voices. They have turned his land into a waste; his cities are charred ruins, without an inhabitant.
text.DRB: The lions have roared upon him, & have given their voice, they have made his land a wilderness: his cities are burnt up, & there is none to dwell in them.

text.NRSV: Moreover, the people of Memphis and Tahpanhes have broken the crown of your head.
text.Rotherham: Even the sons of Noph and Tahpanhes have been crushing the crown of the head.
text.KJV: Also the children of Noph and Tahapanes have broken the crown of thy head.
text.NABRE: Yes, the people of Memphis and Tahpanhes shave the crown of your head.
text.DRB: The children also of Memphis and Taphnes have deflowered thee, even to the crown of the head.

text.NRSV: Have you not brought this upon yourself by forsaking the Lord your God, while he led you in the way?
text.Rotherham: Is not this what thou wast certain to do for thyself,—in that thou didst forsake Yahweh thy God, when he was leading thee by the way?
text.KJV: Hast thou not procured this unto thyself, in that thou hast forsaken the Lord thy God, when he led thee by the way?
text.NABRE: Has not forsaking the Lord, your God, done this to you?
text.DRB: Is not this done to thee, because thou didst forsake the Lord thy God at that time, when he led thee by the way?

text.NRSV: What then do you gain by going to Egypt, to drink the waters of the Nile? Or what do you gain by going to Assyria, to drink the waters of the Euphrates?
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore what hast thou to do with the way to Egypt, to drink the waters of Shihor? Or what hast thou to do with the way to Assyria, to drink the waters of the River [="Euphrates"]?
text.KJV: And now what hast thou to do in the way of Egypt, to drink the waters of Sihor? or what hast thou to do in the way of Assyria, to drink the waters of the river?
text.NABRE: And now, why go to Egypt, to drink the waters of the Nile? Why go to Assyria, to drink the waters of the River?
text.DRB: And now what wilt thou in the way of Ægypt, to drink the troubled water? And what hast thou to do with the way of the Assyrians, to drink the waters of the river?

text.NRSV: Your wickedness will punish you, and your apostasies will convict you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake the Lord your God; the fear of me is not in you, says the Lord God of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Let thy wickedness correct thee And thine apostasies, rebuke thee, Know therefore and see That it is a wicked thing and a bitter, that thou shouldest have forsaken Yahweh thy God, And that the dread of me should not have pertained to thee, Declareth My Lord, Yahweh of hosts.
text.KJV: Thine own wickedness shall correct thee, and thy backslidings shall reprove thee: know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the Lord thy God, and that my fear is not in thee, saith the Lord God of hosts.
text.NABRE: Your own wickedness chastises you, your own infidelities punish you. Know then, and see, how evil and bitter is your forsaking the Lord, your God, And your showing no fear of me, oracle of the Lord, the God of hosts.
text.DRB: Thy malice shall reprove thee, and thine apostasy shall rebuke thee. Know thou, & see that it is an evil & a bitter thing for thee, to have left the Lord thy God, & that my fear is not with thee, saith our Lord the God of hosts.

text.NRSV: For long ago you broke your yoke and burst your bonds, and you said, “I will not serve!” On every high hill and under every green tree you sprawled and played the whore.
text.Rotherham: For, in age-past time, I brake thy yoke, I tare off thy fetters, And thou saidst, I will not transgress,—Nevertheless, on every high hill, and under every green tree, wast thou lying down as an unchaste woman.
text.KJV: For of old time I have broken thy yoke, and burst thy bands; and thou saidst, I will not transgress; when upon every high hill and under every green tree thou wanderest, playing the harlot.
text.NABRE: Long ago you broke your yoke, you tore off your bonds. You said, “I will not serve.” On every high hill, under every green tree, you sprawled and served as a prostitute.
text.DRB: From the beginning thou hast broken my yoke, thou hast burst my bonds, & thou saidst: I will not serve. For on every little high hill, & under every green thick tree thou wast laid down as an harlot.

text.NRSV: Yet I planted you as a choice vine, from the purest stock. How then have you turned degenerate and become a wild vine?
text.Rotherham: Yet, I, planted thee a precious vine, a wholly true seed,—How then didst thou change thyself towards me, into the degenerate plantings of the alien vine?
text.KJV: Yet I had planted thee a noble vine, wholly a right seed: how then art thou turned into the degenerate plant of a strange vine unto me?
text.NABRE: But I had planted you as a choice vine, all pedigreed stock; How could you turn out so obnoxious to me, a spurious vine?
text.DRB: But I planted thee an elect vineyard, all true seed: how then art thou turned unto me into that which is depraved, o strange vineyard?

text.NRSV: Though you wash yourself with lye and use much soap, the stain of your guilt is still before me, says the Lord God.
text.Rotherham: For though thou wash thee with nitre, And take thee much soap, Yet is thine iniquity inscribed before me Declareth My Lord Yahweh.
text.KJV: For though thou wash thee with nitre, and take thee much soap, yet thine iniquity is marked before me, saith the Lord God.
text.NABRE: Even if you scour it with lye, and use much soap, The stain of your guilt is still before me, oracle of the Lord God.
text.DRB: If thou shalt wash thyself with nitre, and multiply to thyself the herb borith, thou art spotted in thine iniquity before me, saith our Lord God.

text.NRSV: How can you say, “I am not defiled, I have not gone after the Baals”? Look at your way in the valley; know what you have done— a restive young camel interlacing her tracks,
text.Rotherham: How canst thou say, I have not defiled myself, After the Baalim, have I not gone? See thy way, in the valley, Own what thou hast done,—A nimble young she-camel, crossing her own ways;
text.KJV: How canst thou say, I am not polluted, I have not gone after Baalim? see thy way in the valley, know what thou hast done: thou art a swift dromedary traversing her ways;
text.NABRE: How can you say, “I am not defiled, I have not pursued the Baals”? Consider your conduct in the Valley, recall what you have done: A skittish young camel, running back and forth,
text.DRB: How sayst thou: I am not polluted, I have not walked after Baalim? See thy ways in the valley, know what thou hast done: a swift courser that rideth his ways.

text.NRSV: a wild ass at home in the wilderness, in her heat sniffing the wind! Who can restrain her lust? None who seek her need weary themselves; in her month they will find her.
text.Rotherham: A wild ass, taught of the desert, In the desire of her soul, she snuffeth the wind, In her occasion, who can turn her back? None who seek her, will weary themselves, In her month, they shall find her!
text.KJV: A wild ass used to the wilderness, that snuffeth up the wind at her pleasure; in her occasion who can turn her away? all they that seek her will not weary themselves; in her month they shall find her.
text.NABRE: a wild donkey bred in the wilderness, Sniffing the wind in her desire— who can restrain her lust? None seeking her need tire themselves; in her time they will find her.
text.DRB: The wild Ass accustomed to the wilderness in the desire of his soul, hath drawn the wind of his love: none shall turn her away: all that seek her shall not fail: in her monthly flowers they shall find her.

text.NRSV: Keep your feet from going barefoot and your throat from thirst. But you said, “It is hopeless, for I have loved strangers, and after them I will go.”
text.Rotherham: Withhold thy foot from being unshod, And thy throat from thirst! But thou saidst Hopeless! No! for I love foreigners and after them, will I go.
text.KJV: Withhold thy foot from being unshod, and thy throat from thirst: but thou saidst, There is no hope: no; for I have loved strangers, and after them will I go.
text.NABRE: Stop wearing out your feet and parching your throat! But you say, “No use! No! How I love these strangers, after them I must go.”
text.DRB: Stay thy foot from nakedness, and thy throat from thirst. And thou saidst: I have despaired, no, I will not do it: for I have loved strangers, and I will walk after them.

text.NRSV: As a thief is shamed when caught, so the house of Israel shall be shamed— they, their kings, their officials, their priests, and their prophets,
text.Rotherham: As the shame of a thief when he is found, So, hath been put to shame, the house of Israel,—They, their kings their princes, and their priests and their prophets:
text.KJV: As the thief is ashamed when he is found, so is the house of Israel ashamed; they, their kings, their princes, and their priests, and their prophets,
text.NABRE: As the thief is shamed when caught, so shall the house of Israel be shamed: They, their kings, their princes, their priests and their prophets;
text.DRB: As the thief is confounded when he is taken, so is the house of Israel confounded, they and their kings, the princes and priests, and their prophets,

text.NRSV: who say to a tree, “You are my father,” and to a stone, “You gave me birth.” For they have turned their backs to me, and not their faces. But in the time of their trouble they say, “Come and save us!”
text.Rotherham: Saying, to a tree, My father, art thou! And to a stone, Thou, didst give us birth, For they have turned unto me the back and not the face,—But in the time of their calamity, they will say, Arise, and save us!
text.KJV: Saying to a stock, Thou art my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth: for they have turned their back unto me, and not their face: but in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise, and save us.
text.NABRE: They say to a piece of wood, “You are my father,” and to a stone, “You gave me birth.” They turn their backs to me, not their faces; yet in their time of trouble they cry out, “Rise up and save us!”
text.DRB: saying to wood: Thou art my father: and to stone: Thou hast begotten me: they have turned the back to me, & not the face: and in the time of their affliction they will say: Arise, and deliver us.

text.NRSV: But where are your gods that you made for yourself? Let them come, if they can save you, in the time of your trouble; for you have as many gods as you have towns, O Judah.
text.Rotherham: Where then are thy gods which thou hast made for thyself? Let them arise, if they can save thee in the time of thy calamity,—For according to the number of thy cities, have become thy gods O Judah.
text.KJV: But where are thy gods that thou hast made thee? let them arise, if they can save thee in the time of thy trouble: for according to the number of thy cities are thy gods, O Judah.
text.NABRE: Where are the gods you made for yourselves? Let them rise up! Will they save you in your time of trouble? For as numerous as your cities are your gods, O Judah! And as many as the streets of Jerusalem are the altars you have set up for Baal.
text.DRB: Where are the gods, whom thou hast made to thee? Let them arise and deliver thee in the time of thine affliction: for according to the number of thy cities were thy gods, o Juda.

text.NRSV: Why do you complain against me? You have all rebelled against me, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, should ye plead against me, All of you, have transgressed against me Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Wherefore will ye plead with me? ye all have transgressed against me, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Why are you arguing with me? You have all rebelled against me—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: What will you contend with me in judgement? You have all forsaken me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: In vain I have struck down your children; they would not take correction. Your own sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.
text.Rotherham: In vain, have I smitten your children, Correction, have they not accepted,—Your sword hath devoured your prophets, as a lion that destroyeth.
text.KJV: In vain have I smitten your children; they received no correction: your own sword hath devoured your prophets, like a destroying lion.
text.NABRE: In vain I struck your children; correction they did not take. Your sword devoured your prophets like a ravening lion.
text.DRB: In vain have I strucken your children, they have not received discipline: your sword hath devoured your Prophets, as a wasting lion is your generation.

text.NRSV: And you, O generation, behold the word of the Lord! Have I been a wilderness to Israel, or a land of thick darkness? Why then do my people say, “We are free, we will come to you no more”?
text.Rotherham: O generation see, ye, the word of Yahweh, A desert, became I unto Israel? Or a land of thick darkness? Wherefore, have my people said, We have roved about, We will not come in any more unto thee?
text.KJV: O generation, see ye the word of the Lord. Have I been a wilderness unto Israel? a land of darkness? wherefore say my people, We are lords; we will come no more unto thee?
text.NABRE: You people of this generation, consider the word of the Lord: Have I become a wilderness to Israel, a land of gloom? Why then do my people say, “We have moved on, we will not come to you any more”?
text.DRB: See ye the word of our Lord: why, am I become a wilderness to Israel, or a lateward springing land? Why then hath my people said: We have revolted, we will no more come to thee?

text.NRSV: Can a girl forget her ornaments, or a bride her attire? Yet my people have forgotten me, days without number.
text.Rotherham: Can, a virgin, forget, her ornaments,—A bride, her girdle? Yet, my people, have forgotten me, days without number.
text.KJV: Can a maid forget her ornaments, or a bride her attire? yet my people have forgotten me days without number.
text.NABRE: Does a young woman forget her jewelry, a bride her sash? Yet my people have forgotten me days without number.
text.DRB: Will a Virgin forget her ornament, or a bride the stomacher of her breast? But my people hath forgotten me innumerable days.

text.NRSV: How well you direct your course to seek love! So that even to wicked women you have taught your ways.
text.Rotherham: How thou dost make winsome thy way, to seek love! Therefore, even unto wicked women, hast thou taught thy ways.
text.KJV: Why trimmest thou thy way to seek love? therefore hast thou also taught the wicked ones thy ways.
text.NABRE: How well you pick your way when seeking love! In your wickedness, you have gone by ways unclean!
text.DRB: Why dost thou endeavour to shew thy way good to seek love, which moreover also hast taught thy malices to be thy ways.

text.NRSV: Also on your skirts is found the lifeblood of the innocent poor, though you did not catch them breaking in. Yet in spite of all these things
text.Rotherham: Even in thy skirts, is there found, The blood of the lives of the helpless innocents,—Not in the act of breaking in, didst thou find them, yet [the blood is] on all these.
text.KJV: Also in thy skirts is found the blood of the souls of the poor innocents: I have not found it by secret search, but upon all these.
text.NABRE: On your clothing is the life-blood of the innocent, you did not find them committing burglary;
text.DRB: And in thy wings is found the blood of the souls of the poor and innocents? Not in ditches have I found them, but in all places, which before I have mentioned.

text.NRSV: you say, “I am innocent; surely his anger has turned from me.” Now I am bringing you to judgment for saying, “I have not sinned.”
text.Rotherham: Although thou saidst, Because I am innocent, surely hath his anger turned back from me,—Behold me! entering into judgment with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned!
text.KJV: Yet thou sayest, Because I am innocent, surely his anger shall turn from me. Behold, I will plead with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned.
text.NABRE: Nonetheless you say, “I am innocent; at least, his anger is turned away from me.” Listen! I will judge you on that word of yours, “I have not sinned.”
text.DRB: And thou hast said: I am without sin and innocent: and therefore let thy fury be turned away from me. Behold, I will contend with thee in judgement, because thou hast said: I have not sinned.

text.NRSV: How lightly you gad about, changing your ways! You shall be put to shame by Egypt as you were put to shame by Assyria.
text.Rotherham: How vigorously, thou goest about changing thy way! Even of Egypt, shalt thou be ashamed, just as thou wast ashamed of Assyria:
text.KJV: Why gaddest thou about so much to change thy way? thou also shalt be ashamed of Egypt, as thou wast ashamed of Assyria.
text.NABRE: How frivolous you have become in changing your course! By Egypt you will be shamed, just as you were shamed by Assyria.
text.DRB: How vile art thou become exceedingly, iterating thy ways! And thou shalt be confounded of Ægypt, as thou art confounded of Assur.

text.NRSV: From there also you will come away with your hands on your head; for the Lord has rejected those in whom you trust, and you will not prosper through them.
text.Rotherham: Even from this one, shalt thou go forth, with thy hands upon thy head,—For Yahweh hath rejected those in whom thou confidest, and thou shalt not prosper with them.
text.KJV: Yea, thou shalt go forth from him, and thine hands upon thine head: for the Lord hath rejected thy confidences, and thou shalt not prosper in them.
text.NABRE: From there too you will go out, your hands upon your head; For the Lord has rejected those in whom you trust, with them you will have no success.
text.DRB: For from hence also thou shalt go, & thy hands shall be upon thy head: because the Lord hath destroyed thy confidence, and thou shalt have nothing prosperous therein.

text.NRSV: If a man divorces his wife and she goes from him and becomes another man’s wife, will he return to her? Would not such a land be greatly polluted? You have played the whore with many lovers; and would you return to me? says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He hath said, If a man send away his wife, and she go from him and become another man’s, will he return unto her, again? would not that land be, utterly defiled? And, thou, hast been unchaste with many neighbours, and yet [thinkest] to return unto me! Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: They say, If a man put away his wife, and she go from him, and become another man’s, shall he return unto her again? shall not that land be greatly polluted? but thou hast played the harlot with many lovers; yet return again to me, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 If a man divorces his wife and she leaves him and then becomes the wife of another, Can she return to the first? Would not this land be wholly defiled? But you have played the prostitute with many lovers, and yet you would return to me!—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: It is commonly said: If a man put away his wife, and she departing from him marry another man, will he return to her any more? Shall not that woman be polluted and contaminated? But thou hast committed fornication with many lovers: nevertheless return unto me, saith our Lord, and I will receive thee.

text.NRSV: Look up to the bare heights, and see! Where have you not been lain with? By the waysides you have sat waiting for lovers, like a nomad in the wilderness. You have polluted the land with your whoring and your wickedness.
text.Rotherham: Lift up thine eyes unto the bare heights and see—where thou hast not been unchastely embraced! beside the ways, hast thou sat to them, like the Arabian in the desert,—and hast defiled the land with thine unchastities, and with thy wickedness.
text.KJV: Lift up thine eyes unto the high places, and see where thou hast not been lien with. In the ways hast thou sat for them, as the Arabian in the wilderness; and thou hast polluted the land with thy whoredoms and with thy wickedness.
text.NABRE: Raise your eyes to the heights, and look, where have men not lain with you? Along the roadways you waited for them like an Arabian in the wilderness. You defiled the land by your wicked prostitution.
text.DRB: Lift up thine eyes direct, and see where thou hast not been polluted: thou didst sit in the ways, expecting them as a robber in the wilderness: and thou hast polluted the land in thy fornications, and in thy malices.

text.NRSV: Therefore the showers have been withheld, and the spring rain has not come; yet you have the forehead of a whore, you refuse to be ashamed.
text.Rotherham: Therefore have been withholden the showers, And, the latter rain, hath not come,—Yet the forehead of an unchaste woman, hast thou, Thou hast refused to be ashamed.
text.KJV: Therefore the showers have been withholden, and there hath been no latter rain; and thou hadst a whore’s forehead, thou refusedst to be ashamed.
text.NABRE: Therefore the showers were withheld, the spring rain did not fall. But because you have a prostitute’s brow, you refused to be ashamed.
text.DRB: For which thing the drops of rain were kept back, and there was no lateward shower: thou hadst an harlot's forehead, thou wouldest not blush.

text.NRSV: Have you not just now called to me, “My Father, you are the friend of my youth—
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not from this time, cried unto me, My father! the friend of my youth, art, thou?
text.KJV: Wilt thou not from this time cry unto me, My father, thou art the guide of my youth?
text.NABRE: Even now do you not call me, “My father, you are the bridegroom of my youth?
text.DRB: Therefore at the least from henceforth call me: Thou art my father, the guide of my virginity.

text.NRSV: will he be angry forever, will he be indignant to the end?” This is how you have spoken, but you have done all the evil that you could.
text.Rotherham: Will he maintain his anger to times age-abiding? Will he keep it perpetually? Lo! thou hast spoken thus but hast done wicked things and had thy way!
text.KJV: Will he reserve his anger for ever? will he keep it to the end? Behold, thou hast spoken and done evil things as thou couldest.
text.NABRE: Will he keep his wrath forever, will he hold his grudge to the end?” This is what you say; yet you do all the evil you can.
text.DRB: Why, wilt thou be angry for ever, or wilt thou persevere unto the end? Lo, thou hast spoken, and hast done evils, and hast prevailed.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me in the days of King Josiah: Have you seen what she did, that faithless one, Israel, how she went up on every high hill and under every green tree, and played the whore there?
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto me, in the days of Josiah the king, Hast thou seen what apostate Israel, did? She used to go upon every high mountain and beneath every green tree, and commit unchastity there.
text.KJV: The Lord said also unto me in the days of Josiah the king, Hast thou seen that which backsliding Israel hath done? she is gone up upon every high mountain and under every green tree, and there hath played the harlot.
text.NABRE: Judah and Israel. The Lord said to me in the days of King Josiah: Do you see what rebellious Israel has done? She has gone up every high mountain, and under every green tree she has played the prostitute.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me in the days of Josias the king: Hast thou seen what things the revolter Israel hath done? She hath gone of herself upon every high mountain, and under every thick green tree and hath fornicated there.

text.NRSV: And I thought, “After she has done all this she will return to me”; but she did not return, and her false sister Judah saw it.
text.Rotherham: And I said, after she had been doing all these things, Unto me, shall thou return? and she returned not,—and her treacherous sister Judah saw it!
text.KJV: And I said after she had done all these things, Turn thou unto me. But she returned not. And her treacherous sister Judah saw it.
text.NABRE: And I thought: After she has done all this, she will return to me. But she did not return. Then, even though that traitor her sister Judah, saw
text.DRB: And I said, when she had done all these things: Return to me, & she returned not. And Juda her transgressing sister saw,

text.NRSV: She saw that for all the adulteries of that faithless one, Israel, I had sent her away with a decree of divorce; yet her false sister Judah did not fear, but she too went and played the whore.
text.Rotherham: Though she saw that for all this, apostate Israel having committed adultery, I had sent her away, and had given a scroll of divorcement unto her, yet her treacherous sister Judah feared not, but, she also, went and committed unchastity.
text.KJV: And I saw, when for all the causes whereby backsliding Israel committed adultery I had put her away, and given her a bill of divorce; yet her treacherous sister Judah feared not, but went and played the harlot also.
text.NABRE: that, in response to all the adulteries rebel Israel had committed, I sent her away and gave her a bill of divorce, nevertheless Judah, the traitor, her sister, was not frightened; she too went off and played the prostitute.
text.DRB: because the rebel Israel had played the harlot, I had put her away, and given her a bill of divorce: and Juda her transgressing sister was not afraid, but went and played the harlot also herself.

text.NRSV: Because she took her whoredom so lightly, she polluted the land, committing adultery with stone and tree.
text.Rotherham: Yea though it had come to pass that through the levity of her unchastity, she had defiled the land,—and committed adultery with Stone and with Tree,
text.KJV: And it came to pass through the lightness of her whoredom, that she defiled the land, and committed adultery with stones and with stocks.
text.NABRE: With her casual prostitution, she polluted the land, committing adultery with stone and wood.
text.DRB: And with the facility of her fornication she contaminated the land, played the harlot with stone & wood.

text.NRSV: Yet for all this her false sister Judah did not return to me with her whole heart, but only in pretense, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: yet, in spite of all this, her treacherous sister Judah returned not unto me, with all her heart,—but, falsely, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: And yet for all this her treacherous sister Judah hath not turned unto me with her whole heart, but feignedly, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: In spite of all this, Judah, the traitor, her sister, did not return to me wholeheartedly, but insincerely—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And in all these things Juda her transgressing sister hath not returned to me in her whole heart, but in falsehood, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to me: Faithless Israel has shown herself less guilty than false Judah.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me,—Apostate Israel, hath justified herself,—more than treacherous Judah.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto me, The backsliding Israel hath justified herself more than treacherous Judah.
text.NABRE: Restoration of Israel. Then the Lord said to me: Rebel Israel is more just than traitor Judah.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: The rebellious Israel hath justified her soul, in comparison of the transgressor Juda.

text.NRSV: Go, and proclaim these words toward the north, and say: Return, faithless Israel, says the Lord. I will not look on you in anger, for I am merciful, says the Lord; I will not be angry forever.
text.Rotherham: Go, and proclaim these words, towards the North and say—Return! thou apostate—Israel, Urgeth Yahweh, I will not lower my face against you,—for full of lovingkindness, I am, Declareth Yahweh, I will not maintain [mine anger] unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: Go and proclaim these words toward the north, and say, Return, thou backsliding Israel, saith the Lord; and I will not cause mine anger to fall upon you: for I am merciful, saith the Lord, and I will not keep anger for ever.
text.NABRE: Go, proclaim these words toward the north, and say: Return, rebel Israel—oracle of the Lord — I will not remain angry with you; For I am merciful, oracle of the Lord, I will not keep my anger forever.
text.DRB: Go, and cry these words against the North, and thou shalt say: Return, o rebellious Israel, saith our Lord, and I will not turn away my face from you: because I am holy, saith our Lord, and I will not be angry for ever.

text.NRSV: Only acknowledge your guilt, that you have rebelled against the Lord your God, and scattered your favors among strangers under every green tree, and have not obeyed my voice, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Only, acknowledge thine iniquity, that against Yahweh thy God, hast thou transgressed,—and hast gone hither and thither unto foreigners under every green tree, and unto my voice, ye have not hearkened Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Only acknowledge thine iniquity, that thou hast transgressed against the Lord thy God, and hast scattered thy ways to the strangers under every green tree, and ye have not obeyed my voice, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Only admit your guilt: how you have rebelled against the Lord, your God, How you ran here and there to strangers under every green tree and would not listen to my voice—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: But yet know thou thine iniquity, that thou hast transgressed against the Lord thy God: and thou hast dispersed thy ways to strangers under every thick green tree, and hast not heard my voice, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Return, O faithless children, says the Lord, for I am your master; I will take you, one from a city and two from a family, and I will bring you to Zion.
text.Rotherham: Return, ye apostate sons, Urgeth Yahweh, for, I, am become your husband,—therefore will I take you one of a city, and two of a family, and will bring you to Zion;
text.KJV: Turn, O backsliding children, saith the Lord; for I am married unto you: and I will take you one of a city, and two of a family, and I will bring you to Zion:
text.NABRE: Return, rebellious children—oracle of the Lord — for I am your master; I will take you, one from a city, two from a clan, and bring you to Zion.
text.DRB: Return, o ye revolting children, saith our Lord: because I am your husband: and I will take you one of a city, and two of a kindred, and will bring you into Sion.

text.NRSV: I will give you shepherds after my own heart, who will feed you with knowledge and understanding.
text.Rotherham: and will give you shepherds according to mine own heart,—who will feed you with knowledge and discretion.
text.KJV: And I will give you pastors according to mine heart, which shall feed you with knowledge and understanding.
text.NABRE: I will appoint for you shepherds after my own heart, who will shepherd you wisely and prudently.
text.DRB: And I will give you Pastors according to my heart, and they shall feed you with knowledge and doctrine.

text.NRSV: And when you have multiplied and increased in the land, in those days, says the Lord, they shall no longer say, “The ark of the covenant of the Lord.” It shall not come to mind, or be remembered, or missed; nor shall another one be made.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass when ye shall be multiplied and become fruitful in the land, in those days, Declareth Yahweh, They shall say no more, The ark of the covenant of Yahweh, Neither shall it come up on the heart, Neither shall they remember it, Neither shall they miss it, Neither shall it be made any more.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, when ye be multiplied and increased in the land, in those days, saith the Lord, they shall say no more, The ark of the covenant of the Lord: neither shall it come to mind: neither shall they remember it; neither shall they visit it; neither shall that be done any more.
text.NABRE: When you increase in number and are fruitful in the land— oracle of the Lord — They will in those days no longer say, “The ark of the covenant of the Lord!” They will no longer think of it, or remember it, or miss it, or make another one.
text.DRB: And when you shall be multiplied and increase in the land in those days, saith our Lord, they shall say no more: The Ark of the Testament of our Lord: neither shall it ascend upon their heart, neither shall they be mindful thereof, neither shall it be visited, neither shall that be done any more.

text.NRSV: At that time Jerusalem shall be called the throne of the Lord, and all nations shall gather to it, to the presence of the Lord in Jerusalem, and they shall no longer stubbornly follow their own evil will.
text.Rotherham: At that time, shall they call Jerusalem, The throne of Yahweh, and there shall be gathered unto her all the nations—to the Name of Yahweh, to Jerusalem; and they shall walk no more after the stubbornness of their own wicked heart.
text.KJV: At that time they shall call Jerusalem the throne of the Lord; and all the nations shall be gathered unto it, to the name of the Lord, to Jerusalem: neither shall they walk any more after the imagination of their evil heart.
text.NABRE: At that time they will call Jerusalem “the Lord ’s throne.” All nations will gather together there to honor the name of the Lord at Jerusalem, and they will no longer stubbornly follow their wicked heart.
text.DRB: In that time Jerusalem shall be called the throne of our Lord: & all Gentiles shall be gathered together to it, in the name of our Lord into Jerusalem, & they shall not walk after the perversity of their most wicked heart.

text.NRSV: In those days the house of Judah shall join the house of Israel, and together they shall come from the land of the north to the land that I gave your ancestors for a heritage.
text.Rotherham: In those days, shall the house of Judah go unto the house of Israel,—that they may enter together out of the land of the North, upon the land which I gave as an inheritance unto your fathers.
text.KJV: In those days the house of Judah shall walk with the house of Israel, and they shall come together out of the land of the north to the land that I have given for an inheritance unto your fathers.
text.NABRE: In those days the house of Judah will walk alongside the house of Israel; together they will come from the land of the north to the land which I gave your ancestors as a heritage.
text.DRB: In those days the house of Juda shall go to the house of Israel, & they shall come together from the land of the North to the land which I gave to your fathers.

text.NRSV: I thought how I would set you among my children, and give you a pleasant land, the most beautiful heritage of all the nations. And I thought you would call me, My Father, and would not turn from following me.
text.Rotherham: Though, I myself, had said,—How can I put thee among the sons, And give thee a land to be coveted, An inheritance of beauty, of the hosts of nations? Yet I said, My father, shalt thou call me, And away from me, shalt thou not turn.
text.KJV: But I said, How shall I put thee among the children, and give thee a pleasant land, a goodly heritage of the hosts of nations? and I said, Thou shalt call me, My father; and shalt not turn away from me.
text.NABRE: Conditions for Forgiveness I thought: How I would like to make you my children! So I gave you a pleasant land, the most beautiful heritage among the nations! You would call me, “My Father,” I thought, and you would never turn away from me.
text.DRB: But I said: How shall I make thee as children, and give thee a land worthy to be desired, the goodly inheritance of the hosts of the Gentiles? And I said: Thou shalt call me father, & shalt not cease to walk after me.

text.NRSV: Instead, as a faithless wife leaves her husband, so have you been faithless to me, O house of Israel, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But indeed [as] a wife goeth treacherously from her husband, so, have ye acted treacherously with me, O house of Israel, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Surely as a wife treacherously departeth from her husband, so have ye dealt treacherously with me, O house of Israel, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: But like a woman faithless to her lover, thus have you been faithless to me, house of Israel—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: But as if a woman should contemn her lover, so hath the house of Israel contemned me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: A voice on the bare heights is heard, the plaintive weeping of Israel’s children, because they have perverted their way, they have forgotten the Lord their God:
text.Rotherham: A voice on the bare heights, is heard, The weeping of the supplications of the sons of Israel,—Because they have perverted their way, Have forgotten Yahweh their God.
text.KJV: A voice was heard upon the high places, weeping and supplications of the children of Israel: for they have perverted their way, and they have forgotten the Lord their God.
text.NABRE: A cry is heard on the heights! the plaintive weeping of Israel’s children, Because they have perverted their way, they have forgotten the Lord, their God.
text.DRB: Avoice was heard in the ways, weeping & howling of the children of Israel: because they have made their way unjust, they have forgotten our Lord their God.

text.NRSV: “Return, O faithless children, I will heal your faithlessness.” “Here we come to you; for you are the Lord our God.
text.Rotherham: Return ye apostate sons, I will heal your apostasies! Behold us! we have come unto thee, For thou, art Yahweh our God.
text.KJV: Return, ye backsliding children, and I will heal your backslidings. Behold, we come unto thee; for thou art the Lord our God.
text.NABRE: Return, rebellious children! I will heal your rebellions. “Here we are! We belong to you, for you are the Lord, our God.
text.DRB: Return ye revolting children, & I will heal your revoltings. Behold we come to thee: for thou art the Lord our God.

text.NRSV: Truly the hills are a delusion, the orgies on the mountains. Truly in the Lord our God is the salvation of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Surely, to falsehood, pertain the hills The noisy throng on the mountains,—Surely, in Yahweh our God, is the salvation of Israel!
text.KJV: Truly in vain is salvation hoped for from the hills, and from the multitude of mountains: truly in the Lord our God is the salvation of Israel.
text.NABRE: Deceptive indeed are the hills, the mountains, clamorous; Only in the Lord our God is Israel’s salvation.
text.DRB: In very deed the little hills were liars, and the multitude of the mountains: verily in the Lord our God is the salvation of Israel.

text.NRSV: “But from our youth the shameful thing has devoured all for which our ancestors had labored, their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.
text.Rotherham: But, the Shameful thing, hath devoured the labour of our fathers from our youth: Their flocks, and their herds, their sons and their daughters.
text.KJV: For shame hath devoured the labour of our fathers from our youth; their flocks and their herds, their sons and their daughters.
text.NABRE: The shameful thing has devoured our ancestors’ worth from our youth, Their sheep and cattle, their sons and daughters.
text.DRB: Confusion hath eaten the labour of our fathers from our youth, their flocks, and their herds, their sons and their daughters.

text.NRSV: Let us lie down in our shame, and let our dishonor cover us; for we have sinned against the Lord our God, we and our ancestors, from our youth even to this day; and we have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God.”
text.Rotherham: We must lie down in our shame And our reproach, be our covering, For, against Yahweh our God, have we sinned, We and our fathers, from our youth even until this day,—Neither have we hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh our God.
text.KJV: We lie down in our shame, and our confusion covereth us: for we have sinned against the Lord our God, we and our fathers, from our youth even unto this day, and have not obeyed the voice of the Lord our God.
text.NABRE: Let us lie down in our shame, let our disgrace cover us, for we have sinned against the Lord, our God, We and our ancestors, from our youth to this day; we did not listen to the voice of the Lord, our God.”
text.DRB: We shall sleep in our confusion, and our ignominy shall cover us, because we have sinned to the Lord our God, we and our fathers from our youth even to this day: and we have not heard the voice of the Lord our God.

text.NRSV: If you return, O Israel, says the Lord, if you return to me, if you remove your abominations from my presence, and do not waver,
text.Rotherham: If thou wilt return, O Israel, Declareth Yahweh, Unto me, mayst thou return,—And if thou wilt remove thine abominations from before me, Then shalt thou not become a wanderer.
text.KJV: If thou wilt return, O Israel, saith the Lord, return unto me: and if thou wilt put away thine abominations out of my sight, then shalt thou not remove.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 If you return, Israel—oracle of the Lord — return to me. If you put your detestable things out of my sight, and do not stray,
text.DRB: If thou wilt return, o Israel, saith our Lord, return to me: if thou wilt take away thy stumbling blocks from my face, thou shalt not be moved.

text.NRSV: and if you swear, “As the Lord lives!” in truth, in justice, and in uprightness, then nations shall be blessed by him, and in him they shall boast.
text.Rotherham: If thou wilt swear, By the life of Yahweh! in faithfulness in justice and in righteousness, Then shall the nations bless themselves in him, And, in him, shall they glory.
text.KJV: And thou shalt swear, The Lord liveth, in truth, in judgment, and in righteousness; and the nations shall bless themselves in him, and in him shall they glory.
text.NABRE: And swear, “As the Lord lives,” in truth, in judgment, and in justice, Then the nations shall bless themselves in him and in him glory.
text.DRB: And thou shalt swear: Our Lord liveth, in truth, & in judgement, and in justice: and the Gentiles shall bless him, & shall praise him.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord to the people of Judah and to the inhabitants of Jerusalem: Break up your fallow ground, and do not sow among thorns.
text.Rotherham: For thus saith Yahweh Unto the men of Judah and unto Jerusalem, Till ye the untilled ground,—And do not sow among thorns.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord to the men of Judah and Jerusalem, Break up your fallow ground, and sow not among thorns.
text.NABRE: For to the people of Judah and Jerusalem, thus says the Lord: Till your untilled ground, and do not sow among thorns.
text.DRB: For thus saith our Lord to the man of Juda, and to Jerusalem: Make unto you new fallow ground, & sow not upon thorns:

text.NRSV: Circumcise yourselves to the Lord, remove the foreskin of your hearts, O people of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, or else my wrath will go forth like fire, and burn with no one to quench it, because of the evil of your doings.
text.Rotherham: Circumcise yourselves unto Yahweh So shall ye remove the impurity of your heart, ye men of Judah and ye inhabitants of Jerusalem,—Lest mine indignation, go forth as fire, and burn and there be none to quench it, Because of the wickedness of your doings.
text.KJV: Circumcise yourselves to the Lord, and take away the foreskins of your heart, ye men of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem: lest my fury come forth like fire, and burn that none can quench it, because of the evil of your doings.
text.NABRE: Be circumcised for the Lord, remove the foreskins of your hearts, people of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem; Or else my anger will break out like fire, and burn so that no one can quench it, because of your evil deeds.
text.DRB: be circumcised to our Lord, and take away the prepuces of your hearts, ye men of Juda, and inhabitants of Jerusalem: lest perhaps mine indignation go forth as fire, and be kindled, & there be none that can quench it: because of the malice of your cogitations.

text.NRSV: Declare in Judah, and proclaim in Jerusalem, and say: Blow the trumpet through the land; shout aloud and say, “Gather together, and let us go into the fortified cities!”
text.Rotherham: Declare ye in Judah And in Jerusalem, let it be heard, And say, Blow ye a horn in the land,—Cry, with frill voice And say, Gather yourselves together, And let us enter the defenced cities.
text.KJV: Declare ye in Judah, and publish in Jerusalem; and say, Blow ye the trumpet in the land: cry, gather together, and say, Assemble yourselves, and let us go into the defenced cities.
text.NABRE: The Invasion from the North Proclaim it in Judah, in Jerusalem announce it; Blow the trumpet throughout the land, call out, “Fill the ranks!” Say, “Assemble, let us march to the fortified cities.”
text.DRB: Declare ye in Juda, and make it heard in Jerusalem: speak, and sound with the trumpet in the land: cry strongly, and say: Assemble yourselves, & let us enter into the fenced cities,

text.NRSV: Raise a standard toward Zion, flee for safety, do not delay, for I am bringing evil from the north, and a great destruction.
text.Rotherham: Lift up an ensign—Zion-ward, Bring into safety, do not tarry,—For, calamity, am, I, bringing in from the North, Even, a great destruction:
text.KJV: Set up the standard toward Zion: retire, stay not: for I will bring evil from the north, and a great destruction.
text.NABRE: Raise the signal—to Zion! Seek refuge! Don’t stand there! Disaster I bring from the north, and great destruction.
text.DRB: lift up the sign in Sion. Take courage, stand not, because I do bring evil from the North, & great destruction.

text.NRSV: A lion has gone up from its thicket, a destroyer of nations has set out; he has gone out from his place to make your land a waste; your cities will be ruins without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: There hath come up a lion out of his thicket, Yea, a destroyer of nations—hath set forward, hath come forth out of his place,—To make thy land a desolation, Thy cities, shall fall in ruins, so as to have no inhabitant.
text.KJV: The lion is come up from his thicket, and the destroyer of the Gentiles is on his way; he is gone forth from his place to make thy land desolate; and thy cities shall be laid waste, without an inhabitant.
text.NABRE: Up comes the lion from its lair, the destroyer of nations has set out, has left its place, To turn your land into a desolation, your cities into an uninhabited waste.
text.DRB: The Lion is come up out of his den, and the robber of the Gentiles hath lifted up himself: he is come forth out of his place, that he may make thy land as a wilderness: thy cities shall be wasted, remaining without an inhabiter.

text.NRSV: Because of this put on sackcloth, lament and wail: “The fierce anger of the Lord has not turned away from us.”
text.Rotherham: On this account, gird you with sackcloth, lament and howl,—Because the glow of the anger of Yahweh hath not turned from us.
text.KJV: For this gird you with sackcloth, lament and howl: for the fierce anger of the Lord is not turned back from us.
text.NABRE: So put on sackcloth, mourn and wail: “The blazing anger of the Lord has not turned away from us.”
text.DRB: For this gird yourselves with cloths of hair, mourn and howl: because the wrath of the fury of our Lord is not turned away from us.

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord, courage shall fail the king and the officials; the priests shall be appalled and the prophets astounded.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, Declareth Yahweh, That the courage of the king shall fail, And the courage of the princes,—And the priests shall be astonished, And, the prophets, shall be amazed.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass at that day, saith the Lord, that the heart of the king shall perish, and the heart of the princes; and the priests shall be astonished, and the prophets shall wonder.
text.NABRE: In that day—oracle of the Lord — The king will lose heart, and the princes; the priests will be horrified, and the prophets stunned.
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day, saith our Lord: The heart of the king shall perish, & the heart of the Princes: and the Priests shall be astonished, and the Prophets shall be amazed.

text.NRSV: Then I said, “Ah, Lord God, how utterly you have deceived this people and Jerusalem, saying, ‘It shall be well with you,’ even while the sword is at the throat!”
text.Rotherham: Then said I,—Ah! My Lord Yahweh! Surely, thou hast suffered this people and Jerusalem to be beguiled, saying, Peace, shall ye have,—whereas the sword shall reach unto the soul.
text.KJV: Then said I, Ah, Lord God! surely thou hast greatly deceived this people and Jerusalem, saying, Ye shall have peace; whereas the sword reacheth unto the soul.
text.NABRE: “Ah! Lord God,” they will say, “You really did deceive us When you said: You shall have peace, while the sword was at our very throats.”
text.DRB: And I said: Alas, alas, alas, o Lord God, hast thou then deceived this people & Jerusalem, saying: You shall have peace: and behold the sword hath come even to the soul?

text.NRSV: At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem: A hot wind comes from me out of the bare heights in the desert toward my poor people, not to winnow or cleanse—
text.Rotherham: At that time, shall it be said of this people and of Jerusalem,—The sharp wind of the bare heights in the desert, cometh towards the daughter of my people,—not to winnow nor to cleanse.
text.KJV: At that time shall it be said to this people and to Jerusalem, A dry wind of the high places in the wilderness toward the daughter of my people, not to fan, nor to cleanse,
text.NABRE: At that time it will be said to this people and to Jerusalem, A scorching wind from the bare heights comes through the wilderness toward my daughter, the people. Not to winnow, not to cleanse,
text.DRB: At that time it shall be said to this people, & to Jerusalem: A burning wind in the ways, that are in the desert of the way of the daughter of my people, not to fan and to purge.

text.NRSV: a wind too strong for that. Now it is I who speak in judgment against them.
text.Rotherham: A wind too strong for these, cometh in for me. Now, will, I also, pronounce sentences upon them:
text.KJV: Even a full wind from those places shall come unto me: now also will I give sentence against them.
text.NABRE: a strong wind from there comes at my bidding. Now I too pronounce sentence upon them.
text.DRB: Afull spirit from these shall come to me: and now I will speak my judgement with them.

text.NRSV: Look! He comes up like clouds, his chariots like the whirlwind; his horses are swifter than eagles— woe to us, for we are ruined!
text.Rotherham: Lo! like clouds, shall he come up, Even as a storm-wind, his chariots, Swifter than eagles, his horses,—Woe to us for we are laid waste!
text.KJV: Behold, he shall come up as clouds, and his chariots shall be as a whirlwind: his horses are swifter than eagles. Woe unto us! for we are spoiled.
text.NABRE: See! like storm clouds he advances, like a whirlwind, his chariots; Swifter than eagles, his horses: “Woe to us! we are ruined.”
text.DRB: Behold he shall come in a cloud, and his chariots as a tempest: his horses are swifter than eagles: woe unto us, because we are destroyed.

text.NRSV: O Jerusalem, wash your heart clean of wickedness so that you may be saved. How long shall your evil schemes lodge within you?
text.Rotherham: Wash from wickedness thy heart, O Jerusalem, That thou mayest be saved,—How long, shall lodge within thee thy wicked devices?
text.KJV: O Jerusalem, wash thine heart from wickedness, that thou mayest be saved. How long shall thy vain thoughts lodge within thee?
text.NABRE: Cleanse your heart of evil, Jerusalem, that you may be saved. How long will you entertain wicked schemes?
text.DRB: Wash thy heart from malice, o Jerusalem, that thou mayst be saved: how long shall hurtful cogitations abide in thee?

text.NRSV: For a voice declares from Dan and proclaims disaster from Mount Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: For a voice, declareth from Dan,—And publisheth trouble from the hill country of Ephraim.
text.KJV: For a voice declareth from Dan, and publisheth affliction from mount Ephraim.
text.NABRE: A voice proclaims it from Dan, announces wickedness from Mount Ephraim:
text.DRB: For a voice of one declaring from Dan, and notifying the idol from mount Ephraim.

text.NRSV: Tell the nations, “Here they are!” Proclaim against Jerusalem, “Besiegers come from a distant land; they shout against the cities of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Put ye in mind the nations Lo! publish ye against Jerusalem, Blockaders! are coming in from a land afar off,—And have uttered against the cities of Judah their voice:
text.KJV: Make ye mention to the nations; behold, publish against Jerusalem, that watchers come from a far country, and give out their voice against the cities of Judah.
text.NABRE: “Make this known to the nations, announce it against Jerusalem: Besiegers are coming from the distant land, shouting their war cry against the cities of Judah.”
text.DRB: Say ye to the Gentiles: lo it is heard in Jerusalem, that there come keepers from a far country, and sound their voice upon the cities of Juda.

text.NRSV: They have closed in around her like watchers of a field, because she has rebelled against me, says the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: As the keepers of a field, have they come against her round about,—For against me, hath she rebelled, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: As keepers of a field, are they against her round about; because she hath been rebellious against me, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Like watchers in the fields they surround her, for she has rebelled against me—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: They are set upon her round about, as the keepers of fields: because she hath provoked me to wrath, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Your ways and your doings have brought this upon you. This is your doom; how bitter it is! It has reached your very heart.
text.Rotherham: Thine own way And thine own doings, Have done these things unto thee,—This thy wickedness, Surely it is bitter, Surely it hath reached unto thy heart.
text.KJV: Thy way and thy doings have procured these things unto thee; this is thy wickedness, because it is bitter, because it reacheth unto thine heart.
text.NABRE: Your conduct, your deeds, have done this to you; how bitter is this evil of yours, how it reaches to your very heart!
