text.Rotherham: Why have I seen it? They, panic-struck, are drawing back, Yea, their heroes, are crushed, Yea they have, fled, and not turned,—Terror round about! Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Wherefore have I seen them dismayed and turned away back? and their mighty ones are beaten down, and are fled apace, and look not back: for fear was round about, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: What do I see? Are they panicking, falling apart? Their warriors are hammered back, They flee headlong never making a stand. Terror on every side— oracle of the Lord!
text.DRB: What then? I saw them fearful, and turning their backs, their valiants slain: they fled in haste, neither looked they back: terror on every side, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: The swift cannot flee away, nor can the warrior escape; in the north by the river Euphrates they have stumbled and fallen.
text.Rotherham: Let not the swift, flee, Nor let the hero, escape,—Northward, beside the river Euphrates, have they stumbled, and fallen.
text.KJV: Let not the swift flee away, nor the mighty man escape; they shall stumble, and fall toward the north by the river Euphrates.
text.NABRE: The swift cannot flee, nor the warrior escape: There up north, on the banks of the Euphrates they stumble and fall.
text.DRB: Let not the swift fly, nor the strong think that he is safe: Toward the north by the river Euphrates they were overcome, and fell down.

text.NRSV: Who is this, rising like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge?
text.Rotherham: Who is it that is like the Nile when it riseth, Like rivers when his waters are tossed?
text.KJV: Who is this that cometh up as a flood, whose waters are moved as the rivers?
text.NABRE: Who is this? Like the Nile, it rears up; like rivers, its waters surge.
text.DRB: Who is this that riseth up as a flood: and as it were of rivers, so his streams do swell?

text.NRSV: Egypt rises like the Nile, like rivers whose waters surge. It said, “I will rise, I will cover the earth, I will destroy cities and their inhabitants.”
text.Rotherham: Egypt was like the Nile when it riseth, And like rivers, when the waters are tossed: So he said—I will rise, I will cover the earth, I will destroy the city, and the dwellers therein.
text.KJV: Egypt riseth up like a flood, and his waters are moved like the rivers; and he saith, I will go up, and will cover the earth; I will destroy the city and the inhabitants thereof.
text.NABRE: Egypt rears up like the Nile, like rivers, its waters surge. “I will rear up,” it says, “and cover the earth, destroying the city and its people.
text.DRB: Ægypt riseth up like a flood, & the waves thereof shall be moved as rivers, & shall say: Rising up I will cover the earth: I will destroy city, and the inhabitants thereof.

text.NRSV: Advance, O horses, and dash madly, O chariots! Let the warriors go forth: Ethiopia and Put who carry the shield, the Ludim, who draw the bow.
text.Rotherham: Mount the horses, And drive the chariots madly on, So let the heroes, go forth,—Ethiopians and Libyans that grasp the buckler, And Lydians that grasp—that tread—the bow, But, that day, belongeth to My Lord Yahweh of hosts—
text.KJV: Come up, ye horses; and rage, ye chariots; and let the mighty men come forth; the Ethiopians and the Libyans, that handle the shield; and the Lydians, that handle and bend the bow.
text.NABRE: Forward, horses! charge, chariots! March forth, warriors, Cush and Put, bearing shields, Archers of Lud, stretching bows!”
text.DRB: Get ye up on horses, & in chariots, & let the valiants come forth, Æthiopia, and the Libyans holding the shield, and the Lydeans taking, and shooting arrows.

text.NRSV: That day is the day of the Lord God of hosts, a day of retribution, to gain satisfaction from his foes. The sword shall devour and be sated, and drink its fill of their blood. For the Lord God of hosts holds a sacrifice in the north country by the river Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: A day of avenging, to avenge him of his adversaries, When the sword shall devour and be filled, and be sated with their blood,—For, a sacrifice, hath My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, In the land of the North Towards the river Euphrates.
text.KJV: For this is the day of the Lord God of hosts, a day of vengeance, that he may avenge him of his adversaries: and the sword shall devour, and it shall be satiate and made drunk with their blood: for the Lord God of hosts hath a sacrifice in the north country by the river Euphrates.
text.NABRE: Today belongs to the Lord God of hosts, a day of vengeance, vengeance on his foes! The sword devours and is sated, drunk with their blood: for the Lord God of hosts holds a sacrifice in the land of the north, on the River Euphrates.
text.DRB: And that day of our Lord the God of hosts, is a day of revenge, that they may take vengeance of his enemies: the sword shall devour, and be filled, and shall be drunken with their blood: for the victim of our Lord the God of hosts is in the Land of the North by the river Euphrates.

text.NRSV: Go up to Gilead, and take balm, O virgin daughter Egypt! In vain you have used many medicines; there is no healing for you.
text.Rotherham: Go up to Gilead and fetch balsam, O virgin daughter of Egypt! In vain, hast thou multiplied remedies, Healing, there is none for thee.
text.KJV: Go up into Gilead, and take balm, O virgin, the daughter of Egypt: in vain shalt thou use many medicines; for thou shalt not be cured.
text.NABRE: Go up to Gilead, procure balm, Virgin daughter Egypt! No use to multiply remedies; for you there is no healing.
text.DRB: Go up into Galaad, & take resin o virgin the daughter of Ægypt: thou doest in vain multiply medicines, there shall not be health to thee.

text.NRSV: The nations have heard of your shame, and the earth is full of your cry; for warrior has stumbled against warrior; both have fallen together.
text.Rotherham: The nations have heard of thy disgrace, And, thine outcry, hath filled the earth,—For hero against hero, have they stumbled, Together, have fallen, the twain!
text.KJV: The nations have heard of thy shame, and thy cry hath filled the land: for the mighty man hath stumbled against the mighty, and they are fallen both together.
text.NABRE: The nations hear your cries, your screaming fills the earth. Warrior stumbles against warrior, both collapse together.
text.DRB: The Gentiles have heard thine ignominy, and thine howling hath filled the earth: because the strong hath stumbled against the strong, and both are fallen together.

text.NRSV: The word that the Lord spoke to the prophet Jeremiah about the coming of King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon to strike the land of Egypt:
text.Rotherham: The word which Yahweh spake unto Jeremiah the prophet,—as to the coming of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon to smite the land of Egypt:—
text.KJV: The word that the Lord spake to Jeremiah the prophet, how Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon should come and smite the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: The word that the Lord spoke to Jeremiah the prophet when Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon, came to attack the land of Egypt:
text.DRB: The word that our Lord spake to Jeremy the Prophet, concerning this, that Nabuchodonosor king of Babylon should come and strike the Land of Ægypt:

text.NRSV: Declare in Egypt, and proclaim in Migdol; proclaim in Memphis and Tahpanhes; Say, “Take your stations and be ready, for the sword shall devour those around you.”
text.Rotherham: Tell ye it in Egypt, And let it be heard in Migdol, Yea let it be heard in Noph, and in Tahpanhes,—Say ye—Stand thou forth, and prepare thyself, For a sword hath devoured round about thee.
text.KJV: Declare ye in Egypt, and publish in Migdol, and publish in Noph and in Tahpanhes: say ye, Stand fast, and prepare thee; for the sword shall devour round about thee.
text.NABRE: Proclaim in Egypt, announce in Migdol, announce in Memphis and Tahpanhes! Say: Fall in, get ready, the sword has devoured your neighbors.
text.DRB: Tell Ægypt, and make it heard in Magdal, and let it sound in Memphis, and in Taphnis: Say ye: Stand, and prepare thyself: because the sword shall devour those things, that be round about thee.

text.NRSV: Why has Apis fled? Why did your bull not stand? Because the Lord thrust him down.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore have thy valiant ones been laid prostrate? He hath made no stand, because, Yahweh, hath driven him back:
text.KJV: Why are thy valiant men swept away? they stood not, because the Lord did drive them.
text.NABRE: Why has Apis fled? Your champion did not stand, Because the Lord thrust him down;
text.DRB: Why is thy valiant become rotten? He stood not: because our Lord hath overthrown him.

text.NRSV: Your multitude stumbled and fell, and one said to another, “Come, let us go back to our own people and to the land of our birth, because of the destroying sword.”
text.Rotherham: Hath made many a one to be stumbling, Yea fallen is every one against his neighbour, So they have said—Arise and let us return to our own people, And unto the land of our birth, From the face of the sword of the oppressor.
text.KJV: He made many to fall, yea, one fell upon another: and they said, Arise, and let us go again to our own people, and to the land of our nativity, from the oppressing sword.
text.NABRE: he stumbled repeatedly then collapsed. They said to each other, “Get up! We must return to our own people, To the land of our birth, away from the destroying sword.”
text.DRB: He hath multiplied them that fall, and man hath fallen against his neighbour, and they shall say: Arise, and let us return to our people, and to the Land of our nativity, from the face of the sword of the dove.

text.NRSV: Give Pharaoh, king of Egypt, the name “Braggart who missed his chance.”
text.Rotherham: Proclaim ye a name,—Pharaoh, king of Egypt—a Sound! He hath overstepped the time appointed!
text.KJV: They did cry there, Pharaoh king of Egypt is but a noise; he hath passed the time appointed.
text.NABRE: Give Pharaoh, king of Egypt, the name “Braggart-missed-his-chance.”
text.DRB: Call ye the name of Pharao the king of Ægypt, Tumult, Time hath brought it.

text.NRSV: As I live, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts, one is coming like Tabor among the mountains, and like Carmel by the sea.
text.Rotherham: As I live, saith the King, Yahweh of hosts, is his name: Though Like Tabor among mountains, Like Carmel, Into the sea shall he go!
text.KJV: As I live, saith the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts, Surely as Tabor is among the mountains, and as Carmel by the sea, so shall he come.
text.NABRE: As I live, says the King whose name is Lord of hosts, Like Tabor above mountains, like Carmel above the sea, he comes.
text.DRB: I live (saith the King, the Lord of hosts is his name) that as Thabor in the mountains, and as Carmel in the sea, he shall come.

text.NRSV: Pack your bags for exile, young woman dwelling in Egypt! For Memphis shall become a desert, a ruin, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: Baggage for captivity, prepare thee, O inhabitress, daughter of Egypt; For, Noph, shall become a desolation, And be burned without inhabitant.
text.KJV: O thou daughter dwelling in Egypt, furnish thyself to go into captivity: for Noph shall be waste and desolate without an inhabitant.
text.NABRE: Pack your bags for exile, enthroned daughter Egypt; Memphis shall become a wasteland, an empty ruin.
text.DRB: Make ye vessels of transmigration o daughter inhabitant of Ægypt: because Memphis shall be in desolation, and shall be forsaken, and shall be inhabitable.

text.NRSV: A beautiful heifer is Egypt, but a gadfly from the north has come upon her.
text.Rotherham: A calf of great beauty, is Egypt: The gad-fly, out of the North, cometh—cometh.
text.KJV: Egypt is like a very fair heifer, but destruction cometh; it cometh out of the north.
text.NABRE: Egypt is a beautiful heifer, a horsefly from the north keeps coming.
text.DRB: Ægypt a trim and beautiful heifer: a pricker from the North shall come to her.

text.NRSV: Even her mercenaries in her midst are like fatted calves; they too have turned and fled together, they did not stand; for the day of their calamity has come upon them, the time of their punishment.
text.Rotherham: Even her hirelings in her midst are like fatted calves, For, even they, have turned—have fled at once! have made no stand! For, their day of doom, hath come upon them, The time of their visitation.
text.KJV: Also her hired men are in the midst of her like fatted bullocks; for they also are turned back, and are fled away together: they did not stand, because the day of their calamity was come upon them, and the time of their visitation.
text.NABRE: Even the mercenaries in her ranks are like fattened calves; They too turn and flee together— they do not stand their ground, For their day of ruin comes upon them, their time of punishment.
text.DRB: Her hirelings also that conversed in the midst of her, as fatted calves are turned, and are fled together, neither could they stand: because the day of their slaughter came upon them, the time of their visitation.

text.NRSV: She makes a sound like a snake gliding away; for her enemies march in force, and come against her with axes, like those who fell trees.
text.Rotherham: Her noise, like a serpent, departeth,—For, with a force, they advance, And with axes, have they come against her, like them who fell trees:
text.KJV: The voice thereof shall go like a serpent; for they shall march with an army, and come against her with axes, as hewers of wood.
text.NABRE: Her voice is like a snake! Yes, they come in force; They attack her with axes, like those who fell trees.
text.DRB: Her voice shall sound as if it were of brass, because they shall hasten with an army, & with axes they shall come to her, as it were cutting trees.

text.NRSV: They shall cut down her forest, says the Lord, though it is impenetrable, because they are more numerous than locusts; they are without number.
text.Rotherham: They have cut down her forest Declareth Yahweh, Surely he cannot be searched out,—For they have outnumbered locusts, and cannot be counted.
text.KJV: They shall cut down her forest, saith the Lord, though it cannot be searched; because they are more than the grasshoppers, and are innumerable.
text.NABRE: They cut down her forest—oracle of the Lord — impenetrable though it be; More numerous than locusts, they cannot be counted.
text.DRB: They have cut down her forest, saith our Lord, which cannot be counted: they are multiplied above locusts, and are without number.

text.NRSV: Daughter Egypt shall be put to shame; she shall be handed over to a people from the north.
text.Rotherham: Put to shame is the daughter of Egypt,—She hath been delivered into the hand of the people of the North.
text.KJV: The daughter of Egypt shall be confounded; she shall be delivered into the hand of the people of the north.
text.NABRE: Shamed is daughter Egypt, handed over to a people from the north.
text.DRB: The daughter of Ægypt is confounded, and delivered into the hand of the people of the North.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, said: See, I am bringing punishment upon Amon of Thebes, and Pharaoh, and Egypt and her gods and her kings, upon Pharaoh and those who trust in him.
text.Rotherham: Saith Yahweh of host God of Israel,—Behold me! bringing punishment—against Amon of No, and upon Pharaoh and upon Egypt, and upon her gods and upon her kings,—Even upon Pharaoh, and upon all that trust in him;
text.KJV: The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, saith; Behold, I will punish the multitude of No, and Pharaoh, and Egypt, with their gods, and their kings; even Pharaoh, and all them that trust in him:
text.NABRE: The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, has said: See! I will punish Amon of Thebes and Egypt, gods, kings, Pharaoh, and those who trust in him.
text.DRB: The Lord of hosts the God of Israel hath said: Behold I will visit upon the tumult of Alexandria, and upon Pharao, and upon Ægypt, and upon her gods, and upon her kings, and upon Pharao, and upon them that trust in him.

text.NRSV: I will hand them over to those who seek their lives, to King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon and his officers. Afterward Egypt shall be inhabited as in the days of old, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver them—Into the hand of them who are seeking their life, Even into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon And into the hand of his servants,—And after that, shall it be inhabited as in the days of old Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will deliver them into the hand of those that seek their lives, and into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants: and afterward it shall be inhabited, as in the days of old, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will hand them over to those who seek their lives, to Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon, and to his officers. But later, Egypt shall be inhabited again, as in days of old—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will give them into the hand of them that seek their life, and into the hand of Nabuchodonosor king of Babylon, and into the hand of his servants: and after these things it shall be inhabited as in the days of old, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: But as for you, have no fear, my servant Jacob, and do not be dismayed, O Israel; for I am going to save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease, and no one shall make him afraid.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, do not fear, O my Servant Jacob Nor be dismayed O Israel, For behold me! Saving thee from afar, And thy seed from the land of their captivity,—So shall Jacob return and be quiet and shall rest, With none to occasion alarm.
text.KJV: But fear not thou, O my servant Jacob, and be not dismayed, O Israel: for, behold, I will save thee from afar off, and thy seed from the land of their captivity; and Jacob shall return, and be in rest and at ease, and none shall make him afraid.
text.NABRE: But you, my servant Jacob, do not fear; do not be dismayed, Israel! Listen! I will deliver you from far-off lands; your offspring, from the land of their exile. Jacob shall again find rest, secure, with none to frighten him.
text.DRB: And thou my servant Jacob fear not, and be not thou afraid Israel: because lo I will save thee from a far, and thy seed out of the land of thy captivity: and Jacob shall return, and rest, and prosper: and there shall be none to terrify him.

text.NRSV: As for you, have no fear, my servant Jacob, says the Lord, for I am with you. I will make an end of all the nations among which I have banished you, but I will not make an end of you. I will chastise you in just measure, and I will by no means leave you unpunished.
text.Rotherham: Thou, do not fear, O my Servant Jacob, Urgeth Yahweh, For, with thee, am I: Though I make an end of all the nations whither I have driven thee, Yet of thee, will I not make an end, But will correct thee in measure, And not hold thee, guiltless,
text.KJV: Fear thou not, O Jacob my servant, saith the Lord: for I am with thee; for I will make a full end of all the nations whither I have driven thee: but I will not make a full end of thee, but correct thee in measure; yet will I not leave thee wholly unpunished.
text.NABRE: You, Jacob my servant, must not fear—oracle of the Lord — for I am with you; I will make an end of all the nations to which I have driven you, But of you I will not make an end: I will chastise you as you deserve, I cannot let you go unpunished.
text.DRB: And thou my servant Jacob fear not, saith our Lord: because I am with thee, because I will consume all the nations, to the which I have cast thee out: but thee I will not consume, but I will chasten thee in judgement, neither will I spare thee as innocent.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning the Philistines, before Pharaoh struck down Gaza.
text.Rotherham: So much of the word of Yahweh as came unto Jeremiah the prophet, Against the Philistines,—before Pharaoh smote Gaza:
text.KJV: The word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet against the Philistines, before that Pharaoh smote Gaza.
text.NABRE: Chapter 47 Against the Philistines. The word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the Philistines, before Pharaoh attacked Gaza:
text.DRB: The word of our Lord that was made to Jeremy the Prophet against the Palesthines, before Pharao struck Gaza:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: See, waters are rising out of the north and shall become an overflowing torrent; they shall overflow the land and all that fills it, the city and those who live in it. People shall cry out, and all the inhabitants of the land shall wail.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Lo! waters rising from the North And they shall become a torrent overflowing, Which shall overflow The land and the fulness thereof, The city, and the dwellers therein,—Then shall men make outcry, And all the inhabitants of the land, howl.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord; Behold, waters rise up out of the north, and shall be an overflowing flood, and shall overflow the land, and all that is therein; the city, and them that dwell therein: then the men shall cry, and all the inhabitants of the land shall howl.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: See: waters are rising from the north, to become a torrent in flood; They shall flood the land and all it contains, the cities and their inhabitants. People will howl and wail, every inhabitant of the land.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord: Behold there come up waters from the North, and they shall be as a torrent overflowing, and they shall cover the earth, and the fullness thereof, the city and the inhabitants thereof: men shall cry, and all the inhabitants of the land shall howl

text.NRSV: At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his stallions, at the clatter of his chariots and the rumbling of their wheels, parents do not turn back for children, so feeble are their hands,
text.Rotherham: Because of the sound of the tramping of the hoofs of his chargers, Because of the rushing of his chariots, the rumbling of his wheels Fathers shall not turn, to children, Because of the unnerving of their hands;
text.KJV: At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his strong horses, at the rushing of his chariots, and at the rumbling of his wheels, the fathers shall not look back to their children for feebleness of hands;
text.NABRE: At the noise of the pounding hooves of his steeds, the clanking chariots, the rumbling wheels, Parents do not turn back for their children; their hands hang helpless,
text.DRB: at the noise of the pomp of the armour, and of his men of war, at the commotion of his chariots, and the multitude of his wheels. The fathers have not respected the children, being of dissolute hands,

text.NRSV: because of the day that is coming to destroy all the Philistines, to cut off from Tyre and Sidon every helper that remains. For the Lord is destroying the Philistines, the remnant of the coastland of Caphtor.
text.Rotherham: On account of the day that cometh to lay waste all the Philistines, To cut off from Tyre and Zidon every escaped one that could have helped,—For Yahweh is laying waste the Philistines, The remnant of the Coastland of Caphtor.
text.KJV: Because of the day that cometh to spoil all the Philistines, and to cut off from Tyrus and Zidon every helper that remaineth: for the Lord will spoil the Philistines, the remnant of the country of Caphtor.
text.NABRE: Because of the day that is coming to destroy all the Philistines And cut off from Tyre and Sidon the last of their allies. Yes, the Lord is destroying the Philistines, the remnant from the coasts of Caphtor.
text.DRB: for the coming of the day, wherein all the Philisthiims shall be wasted, and Tyre, and Sidon shall be destroyed with all the rest of their aids. For our Lord hath spoiled the Palesthines, the remnant of the isle of Cappadocia.

text.NRSV: Baldness has come upon Gaza, Ashkelon is silenced. O remnant of their power! How long will you gash yourselves?
text.Rotherham: Baldness hath come, upon Gaza, Silenced is Ashkelon the remnant of their vale,—How long, wilt thou cut thyself?
text.KJV: Baldness is come upon Gaza; Ashkelon is cut off with the remnant of their valley: how long wilt thou cut thyself?
text.NABRE: Baldness is visited upon Gaza, Ashkelon is reduced to silence; Ashdod, remnant of their strength, how long will you gash yourself?
text.DRB: Baldness is come upon Gaza. Ascalon hath held her peace, and the remnant of her valley, how long shalt thou be hewed?

text.NRSV: Ah, sword of the Lord! How long until you are quiet? Put yourself into your scabbard, rest and be still!
text.Rotherham: Ah! thou sword of Yahweh, How long, wilt thou not be quiet? Withdraw into thy scabbard, Rest thyself and be still.
text.KJV: O thou sword of the Lord, how long will it be ere thou be quiet? put up thyself into thy scabbard, rest, and be still.
text.NABRE: Ah! Sword of the Lord! When will you find rest? Return to your scabbard; stop, be still!
text.DRB: O sword of our Lord, how long wilt thou not be quiet? Get into thy scabbard, be cooled, and be still.

text.NRSV: How can it be quiet when the Lord has given it a charge? Against Ashkelon and against the seashore—there he has appointed it.
text.Rotherham: How, can it be quiet, when, Yahweh, hath given it a charge,—against Ashkelon and against the shore of the sea? There, hath he appointed it!
text.KJV: How can it be quiet, seeing the Lord hath given it a charge against Ashkelon, and against the sea shore? there hath he appointed it.
text.NABRE: How can it find rest when the Lord has commanded it? Against Ashkelon and the seacoast, there he has appointed it.
text.DRB: How shall it be quiet when our Lord hath commanded it against Ascalon, and against the countries thereof by the sea side, and there hath made appointment with it?

text.NRSV: Concerning Moab. Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Alas for Nebo, for it is laid waste! Kiriathaim is put to shame, it is taken; the fortress is put to shame and broken down;
text.Rotherham: Of Moab,—Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts God of Israel,—Alas for Nebo, for it is laid waste, put to shame—captured, is Kiriathaim, put to shame is Misgab, and dismayed.
text.KJV: Against Moab thus saith the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel; Woe unto Nebo! for it is spoiled: Kiriathaim is confounded and taken: Misgab is confounded and dismayed.
text.NABRE: Chapter 48 Against Moab. Concerning Moab. Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Ah, Nebo! it is ravaged; Kiriathaim is disgraced, captured; Disgraced and overthrown is the stronghold:
text.DRB: To Moab thus saith the Lord of hosts the God of Israel: Woe upon Nabo, because it is wasted, and confounded: Cariathaim is taken: the strong one is confounded, and hath trembled.

text.NRSV: the renown of Moab is no more. In Heshbon they planned evil against her: “Come, let us cut her off from being a nation!” You also, O Madmen, shall be brought to silence; the sword shall pursue you.
text.Rotherham: No more, is the praise of Moab, In Heshbon, have they devised against her, calamity, Come, and let us cut her off from being a nation,—Even thou, Madmen also, shalt be silenced, After thee, shall march the sword.
text.KJV: There shall be no more praise of Moab: in Heshbon they have devised evil against it; come, and let us cut it off from being a nation. Also thou shalt be cut down, O Madmen; the sword shall pursue thee.
text.NABRE: Moab’s glory is no more. In Heshbon they plot evil against her: “Come! We will put an end to her as a nation.” You, too, Madmen, shall be silenced; you the sword stalks!
text.DRB: There is no more rejoicing in Moab: against Hesebon they have thought evil. Come, and let us destroy it from being a nation. Therefore shalt thou in silence hold thy peace, and the sword shall follow thee.

text.NRSV: Hark! a cry from Horonaim, “Desolation and great destruction!”
text.Rotherham: A voice of outcry, from Horonaim,—Wasting and great destruction!
text.KJV: A voice of crying shall be from Horonaim, spoiling and great destruction.
text.NABRE: Listen! an outcry from Horonaim, “Ruin and great destruction!”
text.DRB: The voice of a cry from Oronaim: waste, and great destruction.

text.NRSV: Moab is broken; her little ones cry out.
text.Rotherham: Moab is broken: Her little ones, have caused to be heard an outcry.
text.KJV: Moab is destroyed; her little ones have caused a cry to be heard.
text.NABRE: “Moab is crushed!” their outcry is heard in Zoar.
text.DRB: Moab is destroyed: proclaim a cry to her children.

text.NRSV: For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping bitterly; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard the distressing cry of destruction.
text.Rotherham: For the ascent of Luhith, with weeping, one ascendeth—with weeping,—For in the descent of Horonaim, the distress of the outcry of destruction, have they heard:
text.KJV: For in the going up of Luhith continual weeping shall go up; for in the going down of Horonaim the enemies have heard a cry of destruction.
text.NABRE: Up the ascent of Luhith they go weeping; At the descent to Horonaim they hear cries of anguish:
text.DRB: For by the ascent of Luith shall the mourner go up in weeping: because in the descent of Oronaim the enemies have heard the howling of destruction:

text.NRSV: Flee! Save yourselves! Be like a wild ass in the desert!
text.Rotherham: Flee deliver your own lives,—Then shall the women be as a shrub in the desert.
text.KJV: Flee, save your lives, and be like the heath in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: “Flee, save your lives! Be like a wild donkey in the wilderness!”
text.DRB: Fly, save your lives: & you shall be as heath in the desert.

text.NRSV: Surely, because you trusted in your strongholds and your treasures, you also shall be taken; Chemosh shall go out into exile, with his priests and his attendants.
text.Rotherham: For seeing thou hast trusted in thy works and in thy treasures, Thou too, shalt be captured,—Then shall Chemosh go forth into captivity, His priests and his princes together.
text.KJV: For because thou hast trusted in thy works and in thy treasures, thou shalt also be taken: and Chemosh shall go forth into captivity with his priests and his princes together.
text.NABRE: Because you trusted in your works and your treasures, you also shall be captured. Chemosh shall go into exile, his priests and princes with him.
text.DRB: For that thou hast had confidence in thy munitions, and in thy treasures, thou also shalt be taken: and Chamos shall go into transmigration, his Priests, and his Princes together.

text.NRSV: The destroyer shall come upon every town, and no town shall escape; the valley shall perish, and the plain shall be destroyed, as the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And the spoiler shall enter into every city, And not, a city, shall escape, Then shall perish the vale Then be destroyed the table-land,—As Yahweh hath said.
text.KJV: And the spoiler shall come upon every city, and no city shall escape: the valley also shall perish, and the plain shall be destroyed, as the Lord hath spoken.
text.NABRE: The destroyer comes upon every city, not a city escapes; Ruined is the valley, wasted the plateau—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the spoiler shall come to every city, and no city shall be saved: and the valleys shall perish, & the champion countries shall be destroyed: because our Lord hath said:

text.NRSV: Set up a tombstone for Moab, for she will surely be laid in ruins; her towns shall become a desolation, with no inhabitant in them.
text.Rotherham: Give wing to Moab, For she must, fly away; And her cities To desolation, shall be turned, With no inhabitant therein.
text.KJV: Give wings unto Moab, that it may flee and get away: for the cities thereof shall be desolate, without any to dwell therein.
text.NABRE: Set up a tombstone for Moab; it will soon become a complete wasteland, Its cities turned into ruins where no one dwells.
text.DRB: Give ye the flower to Moab, because flourishing it shall go out: and her cities shall be desolate, and inhabitable.

text.NRSV: Accursed is the one who is slack in doing the Lord’s work, and accursed is the one who keeps back the sword from bloodshed.
text.Rotherham: Accursed be he that doeth the business of Yahweh carelessly; And, accursed be he that withholdeth his sword, from blood!
text.KJV: Cursed be he that doeth the work of the Lord deceitfully, and cursed be he that keepeth back his sword from blood.
text.NABRE: Cursed are they who do the Lord ’s work carelessly, cursed those who keep their sword from shedding blood.
text.DRB: Cursed be he that doeth the work of our Lord fraudulently: and cursed that stayeth his sword from blood.

text.NRSV: Moab has been at ease from his youth, settled like wine on its dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into exile; therefore his flavor has remained and his aroma is unchanged.
text.Rotherham: Moab, hath been at ease from his youth, And settled is he upon his lees, And hath not been poured out from vessel to vessel, Nor into captivity, hath gone,—For this cause, hath his flavour stood still within him, And his scent is unchanged.
text.KJV: Moab hath been at ease from his youth, and he hath settled on his lees, and hath not been emptied from vessel to vessel, neither hath he gone into captivity: therefore his taste remained in him, and his scent is not changed.
text.NABRE: Moab has been resting from its youth, suspended above its dregs, Never poured from flask to flask, never driven into exile. Thus it retained its flavor, its bouquet is not lost.
text.DRB: Moab hath been fruitful from his youth, and hath rested in his dregs: neither hath he been poured out of vessel into vessel, and hath not gone into transmigration: therefore hath his taste remained in him, and his savour is not changed.

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I shall send to him decanters to tip him over, and empty his vessels, and break his jars in pieces.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When I will send to him tilters and they shall tilt him,—And his vessels, shall they empty, And their jars, shall they dash in pieces.
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will send unto him wanderers, that shall cause him to wander, and shall empty his vessels, and break their bottles.
text.NABRE: Be assured! The days are coming—oracle of the Lord —when I will send him wine-makers to decant the wine; they shall empty its flasks and smash its jars.
text.DRB: Therefore behold the days come, saith our Lord: and I will send unto him those that shall order and dispose of his pots, and they shall overthrow him, and shall empty his vessels, and dash their pots one against another.

text.NRSV: Then Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel, their confidence.
text.Rotherham: Then shall Moab, be ashamed, of, Chemosh,—Just as, the sons of Israel, were ashamed of Bethel their confidence.
text.KJV: And Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Beth–el their confidence.
text.NABRE: Chemosh shall disappoint Moab, just as the house of Israel was disappointed by Bethel, in which they trusted.
text.DRB: And Moab shall be ashamed of Chamos, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel, wherein it had confidence.

text.NRSV: How can you say, “We are heroes and mighty warriors”?
text.Rotherham: How can ye say, Heroes, are we? and Men of might for the war?
text.KJV: How say ye, We are mighty and strong men for the war?
text.NABRE: How can you say, “We are heroes, mighty warriors”?
text.DRB: How say ye: We are valiant, and strong men to fight?

text.NRSV: The destroyer of Moab and his towns has come up, and the flower of his young men has gone down to slaughter, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Spoiled is Moab And her cities, hath he ascended, And the choice of his young men, have gone down to the slaughter,—Declareth The King, Yahweh of hosts, is his Name.
text.KJV: Moab is spoiled, and gone up out of her cities, and his chosen young men are gone down to the slaughter, saith the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: The one who ravages Moab and its cities comes up, the best of its youth go down to slaughter— oracle of the King, whose name is Lord of hosts.
text.DRB: Moab is wasted, and her cities they have cast down: & her chosen young men are gone down into slaughter: saith the King, the Lord of hosts is his name.

text.NRSV: The calamity of Moab is near at hand, and his doom approaches swiftly.
text.Rotherham: Near is the doom of Moab, to come,—And, his calamity, hasteth greatly.
text.KJV: The calamity of Moab is near to come, and his affliction hasteth fast.
text.NABRE: Moab’s ruin is near at hand, its disaster approaches swiftly.
text.DRB: The destruction of Moab is nigh to come: the evil thereof shall come exceeding swiftly.

text.NRSV: Mourn over him, all you his neighbors, and all who know his name; say, “How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff!”
text.Rotherham: Lament ye for him—All that are round about him, and All that know his name,—Say, How is broken—The staff of strength! The rod of beauty!
text.KJV: All ye that are about him, bemoan him; and all ye that know his name, say, How is the strong staff broken, and the beautiful rod!
text.NABRE: Mourn, all you neighbors, all you who know its name! Say: How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff!
text.DRB: Comfort him all ye that are round about him, and all you that know his name, say: How is the strong rod broken, the glorious staff?

text.NRSV: Come down from glory, and sit on the parched ground, enthroned daughter Dibon! For the destroyer of Moab has come up against you; he has destroyed your strongholds.
text.Rotherham: Come down from glory, and sit in thirst, O inhabitress daughter of Dibon; For the spoiler of Moab, hath come up against thee, He hath laid in ruins thy strongholds.
text.KJV: Thou daughter that dost inhabit Dibon, come down from thy glory, and sit in thirst; for the spoiler of Moab shall come upon thee, and he shall destroy thy strong holds.
text.NABRE: Come down from glory, sit on the parched ground, enthroned daughter Dibon; Moab’s destroyer has fallen upon you, has shattered your strongholds.
text.DRB: Come down from thy glory, and sit in drought, o habitation of the daughter of Dibon: Because the waster of Moab shall come up to thee, he shall destroy thy munitions.

text.NRSV: Stand by the road and watch, you inhabitant of Aroer! Ask the man fleeing and the woman escaping; say, “What has happened?”
text.Rotherham: Near the way, take thy stand and keep outlook, O inhabitress of Aroer: Ask—him that fleeth, and Her that hath escaped, Say, What hath happened?
text.KJV: O inhabitant of Aroer, stand by the way, and espy; ask him that fleeth, and her that escapeth, and say, What is done?
text.NABRE: Stand along the road, keep watch, enthroned Aroer; Ask the fleeing man, the escaping woman: ask them what has happened.
text.DRB: Stand in the way, and look o habitation of Aroer: ask of him that flyeth: and say to him that hath escaped: What is chanced?

text.NRSV: Moab is put to shame, for it is broken; wail and cry! Tell it by the Arnon, that Moab is laid waste.
text.Rotherham: Confounded is Moab! For it is broken down! Howl and make outcry,—Tell ye in Arnon, That Moab is spoiled;
text.KJV: Moab is confounded; for it is broken down: howl and cry; tell ye it in Arnon, that Moab is spoiled,
text.NABRE: “Moab is put to shame, destroyed.” Wail and cry out, Proclaim it at the Arnon: “Moab is destroyed!”
text.DRB: Moab is confounded, because he is overcome: howl ye, and cry, declare in Arnon, that Moab is wasted.

text.NRSV: Judgment has come upon the tableland, upon Holon, and Jahzah, and Mephaath,
text.Rotherham: And, judgment, hath come Upon the country of the table-land,—Upon Holon, and Upon Jazer and Upon Mephaath;
text.KJV: And judgment is come upon the plain country; upon Holon, and upon Jahazah, and upon Mephaath,
text.NABRE: Judgment has come upon the plateau: on Holon, Jahzah, and Mephaath,
text.DRB: And judgement is come to the champion country: upon Helon, and upon Jasa, and upon Mephaath,

text.NRSV: and Dibon, and Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Dibon, and Upon Nebo, and Upon Beth-diblathaim;
text.KJV: And upon Dibon, and upon Nebo, and upon Beth–diblathaim,
text.NABRE: on Dibon, Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,
text.DRB: and upon Dibon, and upon Nabo, & upon the house of Deblathaim,

text.NRSV: and Kiriathaim, and Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kiriathaim, and Upon Beth-gamul, and Upon Beth-meon;
text.KJV: And upon Kiriathaim, and upon Beth–gamul, and upon Beth–meon,
text.NABRE: on Kiriathaim, Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,
text.DRB: and upon Cariathaim, and upon Bethgamul, and upon Bethmaon,

text.NRSV: and Kerioth, and Bozrah, and all the towns of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kerioth, and Upon Bozrah,—Yea, upon all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.KJV: And upon Kerioth, and upon Bozrah, and upon all the cities of the land of Moab, far or near.
text.NABRE: on Kerioth and on Bozrah: on all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.DRB: and upon Carioth, and upon Bosra: and upon all the cities of the Land of Moab, that are far, and near.

text.NRSV: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cut off is the horn of Moab, Yea, his arm, is broken,—Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The horn of Moab is cut off, its arm is broken—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Make him drunk, because he magnified himself against the Lord; let Moab wallow in his vomit; he too shall be held in derision.
text.Rotherham: Make ye him drunken, Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself; So shall, Moab, stagger, into his own vomit, And shall become a derision, even he!
text.KJV: Make ye him drunken: for he magnified himself against the Lord: Moab also shall wallow in his vomit, and he also shall be in derision.
text.NABRE: Make him drunk because he set himself over against the Lord; let Moab swim in his vomit and become a laughingstock.
text.DRB: Make him drunken, because he is erected against our Lord: and Moab shall wring his hand in his vomiting, and himself also shall be in derision.

text.NRSV: Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves, that whenever you spoke of him you shook your head?
text.Rotherham: And was it not, a derision, that Israel became to thee? And was it not among thieves, he was found? For, as often as thou didst speak of him, thou didst wag the head!
text.KJV: For was not Israel a derision unto thee? was he found among thieves? for since thou spakest of him, thou skippedst for joy.
text.NABRE: Has Israel not been a laughingstock to you? Was he caught among thieves that you wag your heads whenever you speak of him?
text.DRB: For Israel hath been in derision unto thee: as though thou hadst found him amongst thieves: for thy words therefore, which thou hast spoken against him, thou shalt be led captive.

text.NRSV: Leave the towns, and live on the rock, O inhabitants of Moab! Be like the dove that nests on the sides of the mouth of a gorge.
text.Rotherham: Leave ye the cities and dwell in the cliff, Ye inhabitants of Moab; And become ye as a dove, that maketh her nest in the further-side of the fissure’s mouth.
text.KJV: O ye that dwell in Moab, leave the cities, and dwell in the rock, and be like the dove that maketh her nest in the sides of the hole’s mouth.
text.NABRE: Abandon the cities, take shelter in the crags, inhabitants of Moab. Be like the dove that nests in the walls of a gorge.
text.DRB: Leave the cities, and dwell in the rock ye inhabitants of Moab, and be ye as a dove making her nest in the highest mouth of the hole.

text.NRSV: We have heard of the pride of Moab— he is very proud!— of his loftiness, his pride, and his arrogance, and the haughtiness of his heart.
text.Rotherham: We have heard the arrogance of Moab—proud exceedingly: His loftiness and his arrogance and his majesty, and his elevation of heart!
text.KJV: We have heard the pride of Moab, (he is exceeding proud) his loftiness, and his arrogancy, and his pride, and the haughtiness of his heart.
text.NABRE: We have heard of the pride of Moab, pride beyond bounds: His loftiness, his pride, his scorn, his insolent heart.
text.DRB: We have heard the pride of Moab, he is proud exceedingly: his haughtiness, and arrogancy, and pride, and loftiness of his heart.

text.NRSV: I myself know his insolence, says the Lord; his boasts are false, his deeds are false.
text.Rotherham: I know—Declareth Yahweh His passion—that’tis Unjust! His boastings, that Untrue have they made [them]!
text.KJV: I know his wrath, saith the Lord; but it shall not be so; his lies shall not so effect it.
text.NABRE: I myself know his arrogance—oracle of the Lord — liar in word, liar in deed.
text.DRB: I know, saith our Lord, his boasting: and that the strength thereof is not according to it, neither hath it endeavoured to do according to that which it was able.

text.NRSV: Therefore I wail for Moab; I cry out for all Moab; for the men of Kir-heres I mourn.
text.Rotherham: For this cause over Moab, will I howl, And for all Moab, will I make outcry,—For the men of Kir-heres, must one moan!
text.KJV: Therefore will I howl for Moab, and I will cry out for all Moab; mine heart shall mourn for the men of Kir–heres.
text.NABRE: And so I wail over Moab, over all Moab I cry, over the people of Kir-heres I moan.
text.DRB: Therefore will I wail upon Moab, and to all Moab will I cry, to the men of the earthen wall that lament.

text.NRSV: More than for Jazer I weep for you, O vine of Sibmah! Your branches passed over the sea, they reached as far as Jazer; upon your summer fruits and your vintage the destroyer has fallen.
text.Rotherham: Beyond the weeping of Jazer, will I weep for thee O vine of Sibmah, Thy tendrils, have gone over the sea, Unto the sea of Jazer, have they reached, Upon thy summer fruits And upon thy vintage, the spoiler, hath fallen.
text.KJV: O vine of Sibmah, I will weep for thee with the weeping of Jazer: thy plants are gone over the sea, they reach even to the sea of Jazer: the spoiler is fallen upon thy summer fruits and upon thy vintage.
text.NABRE: More than for Jazer I weep for you, vine of Sibmah. Your tendrils trailed down to the sea, as far as Jazer they stretched. Upon your summer harvest and your vintage, the destroyer has fallen.
text.DRB: Of the mourning of Jazer I will weep to thee, o vineyard of Sabama: thy branches have passed over the sea, they are come even to the sea of Jaser: the spoiler hath violently entered upon thine harvest, and thy vintage.

text.NRSV: Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moab; I have stopped the wine from the wine presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; the shouting is no shout of joy.
text.Rotherham: So shall be withdrawn gladness and exultation—From the fruitful field and From the land of Moab,—And wine from the vats, have I caused to fail, They shall not tread with shouting, The shouting shall be no shouting!
text.KJV: And joy and gladness is taken from the plentiful field, and from the land of Moab; and I have caused wine to fail from the winepresses: none shall tread with shouting; their shouting shall be no shouting.
text.NABRE: Joy and gladness are taken away from the garden land, the land of Moab. I dry up the wine from the wine vats, the treader treads no more, the vintage shout is stilled.
text.DRB: Joy and gladness is taken away out of Carmel, and out of the Land of Moab, and the wine out of the presses I have taken away: the treader of the grape shall not sing the accustomed cheerful note.

text.NRSV: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out; as far as Jahaz they utter their voice, from Zoar to Horonaim and Eglath-shelishiyah. For even the waters of Nimrim have become desolate.
text.Rotherham: From the outcry of Heshbon, Even unto Elealeh Even unto Jahaz, have they given forth their voice, From Zoar even unto Horonaim, the third Eglath,—For, even the waters of Nimrim, to utter desolation, have been turned:
text.KJV: From the cry of Heshbon even unto Elealeh, and even unto Jahaz, have they uttered their voice, from Zoar even unto Horonaim, as an heifer of three years old: for the waters also of Nimrim shall be desolate.
text.NABRE: The cry of Heshbon and Elealeh is heard as far as Jahaz; they call from Zoar to Horonaim and to Eglath-shelishiyah; even the waters of Nimrim turn into a wasteland.
text.DRB: From the cry of Hesebon unto Eleale, and Jasa, they have given their voice: from Segor to Oronaim, an heifer of three years old: the waters also of Nemrim shall be very ill.

text.NRSV: And I will bring to an end in Moab, says the Lord, those who offer sacrifice at a high place and make offerings to their gods.
text.Rotherham: Then will I cause to cease, from Moab, Declareth Yahweh,—him that offereth at a high-place, and him that burneth incense to his gods.
text.KJV: Moreover I will cause to cease in Moab, saith the Lord, him that offereth in the high places, and him that burneth incense to his gods.
text.NABRE: I will leave no one in Moab—oracle of the Lord —to offer burnt offerings on the high place or to make sacrifices to their gods.
text.DRB: And I will take away from Moab, saith our Lord, him that offereth in the excelses, and that sacrificeth to his gods.

text.NRSV: Therefore my heart moans for Moab like a flute, and my heart moans like a flute for the men of Kir-heres; for the riches they gained have perished.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, my heart, for Moab, like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Yea my heart for the men of Kir-heres! like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Because, the abundance he hath gotten is lost!
text.KJV: Therefore mine heart shall sound for Moab like pipes, and mine heart shall sound like pipes for the men of Kir–heres: because the riches that he hath gotten are perished.
text.NABRE: Hence my heart wails like a flute for Moab; my heart wails like a flute for the people of Kir-heres: the wealth they accumulated has perished.
text.DRB: Therefore shall my heart sound to Moab as a shalm: and my heart shall give the sound of shalms to the men of the earthen wall: because he hath done more than he could, therefore have they perished.

text.NRSV: For every head is shaved and every beard cut off; on all the hands are gashes, and on the loins is sackcloth.
text.Rotherham: For, every head, is baldness, And every beard, clipped: Upon all hands, are gashes, And upon the loins, sackcloth
text.KJV: For every head shall be bald, and every beard clipped: upon all the hands shall be cuttings, and upon the loins sackcloth.
text.NABRE: Every head has been shaved bald, every beard cut off; every hand gashed, and all their loins are draped in sackcloth.
text.DRB: For every head baldness, and every beard shall be shaven: in all hands binding together, & upon every back cloth of hair.

text.NRSV: On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation; for I have broken Moab like a vessel that no one wants, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Upon all the housetops of Moab And in her broadways, it is all lamentation,—For I have broken Moab—Like a vessel wherein is no pleasure Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: There shall be lamentation generally upon all the housetops of Moab, and in the streets thereof: for I have broken Moab like a vessel wherein is no pleasure, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: On all the rooftops of Moab and in all its squares there is mourning. I have shattered Moab like a pot that no one wants—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Upon all the house tops of Moab, & in the streets thereof all mourning: because I have broken Moab as an unprofitable vessel, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: How it is broken! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become a derision and a horror to all his neighbors.
text.Rotherham: How it is broken down! They have howled, How hath Moab turned the back, for shame,—So shall Moab become a derision and a terror to all round about him.
text.KJV: They shall howl, saying, How is it broken down! how hath Moab turned the back with shame! so shall Moab be a derision and a dismaying to all them about him.
text.NABRE: How terrified they are, how they wail! How Moab turns its back in shame! Moab has become a laughingstock and a horror to all its neighbors!
text.DRB: How is it overcome, & they have howled? How hath Moab cast down the neck, and is confounded? And Moab shall be in derision, and for an example to all round about him.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: Look, he shall swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Moab;
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh, Lo! as an eagle, shall one dart along,—And shall spread out his wings towards Moab.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord; Behold, he shall fly as an eagle, and shall spread his wings over Moab.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord: Look there! Like an eagle he swoops, spreading his wings over Moab.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord: Behold he shall fly as an eagle, and shall stretch forth his wings to Moab.

text.NRSV: the towns shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moab on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Captured is Kerioth, And the fortresses, are seized,—Then shall the heart of the heroes of Moab become in that day, As the heart of a woman in her pains.
text.KJV: Kerioth is taken, and the strong holds are surprised, and the mighty men’s hearts in Moab at that day shall be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.
text.NABRE: Cities are captured, strongholds seized: On that day the hearts of Moab’s warriors become like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.DRB: Carioth is taken, and munitions are won: and the heart of the valiants of Moab in that day, shall be as the heart of a woman in travail.

text.NRSV: Moab shall be destroyed as a people, because he magnified himself against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall, Moab, be destroyed from being a people,—Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself.
text.KJV: And Moab shall be destroyed from being a people, because he hath magnified himself against the Lord.
text.NABRE: Moab shall be wiped out, a people no more, because it set itself over against the Lord.
text.DRB: And Moab shall cease to be a people: because he hath gloried against our Lord.

text.NRSV: Terror, pit, and snare are before you, O inhabitant of Moab! says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Terror and pit and snare are upon thee O inhabitant of Moab Declareth Yahweh:
text.KJV: Fear, and the pit, and the snare, shall be upon thee, O inhabitant of Moab, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Terror, pit, and trap be upon you, enthroned Moab—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Fear, and pit, & snare upon thee o inhabiter of Moab, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Everyone who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit, and everyone who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For I will bring these things upon Moab in the year of their punishment, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that fleeth from the face of the terror, Shall fall into the pit, And, he that getteth up out of the pit, Shall be captured by the snare,—For I will bring against her—against Moab, The year of their visitation, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: He that fleeth from the fear shall fall into the pit; and he that getteth up out of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for I will bring upon it, even upon Moab, the year of their visitation, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Those fleeing the terror fall into the pit; Those climbing out of the pit are caught in the trap; Ah, yes! I will bring these things upon Moab in the year of their punishment—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: He that shall fly from the face of fear, shall fall into the pit: and he that shall come up out of the pit, shall be taken in the snare: for I will bring upon Moab the year of their visitation, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop exhausted; for a fire has gone out from Heshbon, a flame from the house of Sihon; it has destroyed the foreheads of Moab, the scalps of the tumultuous people.
text.Rotherham: In the shadow of Heshbon, stand strengthless, the fugitives,—For, a fire, hath gone forth out of Heshbon And shall flame out of the midst of Sihon, And shall devour the beard of Moab, And the crown of the head of the proudly tumultuous.
text.KJV: They that fled stood under the shadow of Heshbon because of the force: but a fire shall come forth out of Heshbon, and a flame from the midst of Sihon, and shall devour the corner of Moab, and the crown of the head of the tumultuous ones.
text.NABRE: In Heshbon’s shadow the fugitives stop short, exhausted; For fire blazes up from Heshbon, and flames up from the house of Sihon: It consumes the forehead of Moab, the scalp of the noisemakers.
text.DRB: They stood in the shadow of Hesebon that fled from the snare: because there came a fire out of Hesebon, and a flame out of the midst of Sehon, and it hath devoured part of Moab, and the top of the children of tumult.

text.NRSV: Alas for you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh have perished, for your sons have been taken captive, and your daughters into exile.
text.Rotherham: Woe to thee Moab! Lost are the people of Chemosh,—For thy sons have been taken, into captivity, And thy daughters into captivity.
text.KJV: Woe be unto thee, O Moab! the people of Chemosh perisheth: for thy sons are taken captives, and thy daughters captives.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, Moab! You are finished, people of Chemosh! Your sons are taken into exile, your daughters into captivity.
text.DRB: Woe to thee Moab, thou hast perished, o people of Chamos: because thy sons are taken, and thy daughters into captivity.

text.NRSV: Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days, says the Lord. Thus far is the judgment on Moab.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I bring back the captivity of Moab, In the afterpart of the days, Declareth Yahweh, Thus far, is the sentence of Moab.
text.KJV: Yet will I bring again the captivity of Moab in the latter days, saith the Lord. Thus far is the judgment of Moab.
text.NABRE: Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the days to come—oracle of the Lord. Thus far the judgment on Moab.
text.DRB: And I will convert the captivity of Moab in the last days, saith our Lord. Hitherto the judgements of Moab.

text.NRSV: Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says the Lord: Has Israel no sons? Has he no heir? Why then has Milcom dispossessed Gad, and his people settled in its towns?
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ammon-Thus saith Yahweh: Hath Israel no, sons? Hath he no, heir? Why hath, Malkam, taken possession of Gad, Or have, his people, in the cities thereof, made their dwelling?
text.KJV: Concerning the Ammonites, thus saith the Lord; Hath Israel no sons? hath he no heir? why then doth their king inherit Gad, and his people dwell in his cities?
text.NABRE: Chapter 49 Against the Ammonites. Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says the Lord: Has Israel no sons? none to inherit? Why has Milcom disinherited Gad, why are his people living in its cities?
text.DRB: To the children of Ammon. Thus saith our Lord: Why, hath Israel no children? Or hath he not an heir? Why then hath Melchom by inheritance possessed Gad: and his people dwelt in his cities?

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will cause the battle cry to be heard against Rabbah of the Ammonites; it shall become a desolate mound, and its villages shall be burned with fire; then Israel shall dispossess those who dispossessed him, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh When I will cause to be heard against Rabbah of the sons of Ammon, an alarm of war, So shall she become a mound of desolation, And, her villages, with fire, shall blaze,—Then shall Israel, inherit them who inherited him, Saith Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will cause an alarm of war to be heard in Rabbah of the Ammonites; and it shall be a desolate heap, and her daughters shall be burned with fire: then shall Israel be heir unto them that were his heirs, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Therefore the days are coming—oracle of the Lord — when I will sound the battle alarm against Rabbah of the Ammonites; It shall become a mound of ruins, and its villages destroyed by fire. Israel shall then inherit those who disinherited it— oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Therefore behold the days come saith our Lord: and I will make the noise of battle to be heard upon Rabbath the children of Ammon, and it shall be destroyed into an heap, and her daughters shall be burnt with fire, & Israel shall possess his possessors, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Wail, O Heshbon, for Ai is laid waste! Cry out, O daughters of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth, lament, and slash yourselves with whips! For Milcom shall go into exile, with his priests and his attendants.
text.Rotherham: Howl, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled Make outcry, ye daughters of Rabbah, Gird you with sackcloth, Lament ye and run to and fro among the fences,—For, Malkam, into captivity, shall go, His priests and his princes, together.
text.KJV: Howl, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled: cry, ye daughters of Rabbah, gird you with sackcloth; lament, and run to and fro by the hedges; for their king shall go into captivity, and his priests and his princes together.
text.NABRE: Wail, Heshbon, “The ruin is destroyed!” shriek, villages of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth and lament! Run back and forth in the sheepfolds. For Milcom is going into exile, taking priest and prince with him.
text.DRB: Howl Hesebon, because Hai is wasted. Cry ye daughters of Rabbath, gird yourselves with cloths of hair: mourn and go about by the hedges: because Melchom shall be led into transmigration, his Priests, and his Princes together.

text.NRSV: Why do you boast in your strength? Your strength is ebbing, O faithless daughter. You trusted in your treasures, saying, “Who will dare to attack me?”
text.Rotherham: Why shouldst thou glory in the vales, The flowing of thy vale O apostate daughter? She who is trusting in her treasures, [saying,] Who shall invade, me?
text.KJV: Wherefore gloriest thou in the valleys, thy flowing valley, O backsliding daughter? that trusted in her treasures, saying, Who shall come unto me?
text.NABRE: Why boast in your strength, your ebbing strength, rebellious daughter? Why trust in your treasures, saying, “Who would dare attack me?”
text.DRB: What gloriest thou in the valleys? Thy valley hath flowed away o delicate daughter, which didst trust in thy treasures, & saidst: Who shall come to me?

text.NRSV: I am going to bring terror upon you, says the Lord God of hosts, from all your neighbors, and you will be scattered, each headlong, with no one to gather the fugitives.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! bringing upon thee, terror Declareth My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, From all who are round about thee,—And ye shall be driven out every man before it, And there shall be none to bring home the wanderer;
text.KJV: Behold, I will bring a fear upon thee, saith the Lord God of hosts, from all those that be about thee; and ye shall be driven out every man right forth; and none shall gather up him that wandereth.
text.NABRE: See, I am bringing terror upon you— oracle of the Lord God of hosts— from all around you; You shall be scattered, each in headlong flight, with no one to gather the fugitives.
text.DRB: Behold I will bring terror upon thee, saith our Lord the God of hosts, from all that are round about thee: & you shall be dispersed every one from another's sight, neither shall there be any to gather together them that fly.

text.NRSV: But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But afterwards, will I bring back the captivity of the sons of Ammon, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: And afterward I will bring again the captivity of the children of Ammon, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites—oracle of the Lord. Against Edom.
text.DRB: And after these things I will make the captives of the children of Ammon to return, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of hosts: Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?
text.Rotherham: Of Edom—Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Hath counsel perished from the discerning? Is their wisdom corrupt?
text.KJV: Concerning Edom, thus saith the Lord of hosts; Is wisdom no more in Teman? is counsel perished from the prudent? is their wisdom vanished?
text.NABRE: Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of hosts: Is there no more wisdom in Teman, has counsel perished from the prudent, is their wisdom gone?
text.DRB: To Idumea. Thus saith the Lord of hosts: Why is wisdom no more in Theman? Counsel is perished from the children: their wisdom is become unprofitable.

text.NRSV: Flee, turn back, get down low, inhabitants of Dedan! For I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time when I punish him.
text.Rotherham: Flee ye turn go down deep to dwell, O inhabitant of Dedan,—For the doom of Esau, have I brought in upon him The time of his visitation.
text.KJV: Flee ye, turn back, dwell deep, O inhabitants of Dedan; for I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time that I will visit him.
text.NABRE: Flee, retreat, hide deep for lodging, inhabitants of Dedan: For I bring disaster upon Esau when I come to punish them.
text.DRB: Fly and turn your backs, go down into the gulf ye inhabitants of Dedan: because I have brought the perdition of Esau upon him, the time of his visitation.

text.NRSV: If grape-gatherers came to you, would they not leave gleanings? If thieves came by night, even they would pillage only what they wanted.
text.Rotherham: If, grape-gatherers, came to thee, Would they not leave, gleanings? If, thieves in the night, Would they not have taken away what sufficed them?
text.KJV: If grapegatherers come to thee, would they not leave some gleaning grapes? if thieves by night, they will destroy till they have enough.
text.NABRE: If vintagers came upon you, they would leave no gleanings; If thieves by night, they would destroy as they pleased.
text.DRB: If the grape gatherers had come upon thee, they had not left a cluster: if thieves in the night, they had taken that should suffice them.

text.NRSV: But as for me, I have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his hiding places, and he is not able to conceal himself. His offspring are destroyed, as are his kinsfolk and his neighbors; and he is no more.
text.Rotherham: But, I, have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, And to conceal himself, shall he not be able! Spoiled, are his seed, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.
text.KJV: But I have made Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, and he shall not be able to hide himself: his seed is spoiled, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.
text.NABRE: So I myself will strip Esau; I will uncover his lairs so he cannot hide. Offspring and family are destroyed, neighbors, too; he is no more.
text.DRB: But I have discovered Esau, I have revealed his secrets, and he cannot be concealed: his seed is wasted, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he shall not be.

text.NRSV: Leave your orphans, I will keep them alive; and let your widows trust in me.
text.Rotherham: Leave thy fatherless children I, will preserve them alive,—And thy widows, In me, let them trust.
text.KJV: Leave thy fatherless children, I will preserve them alive; and let thy widows trust in me.
text.NABRE: Leave your orphans behind, I will keep them alive; your widows, let them trust in me.
text.DRB: Leave thy pupils: I will make them live: and thy widows shall hope in me.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, will you be the one to go unpunished? You shall not go unpunished; you must drink it.
text.Rotherham: For, thus saith Yahweh—Lo! they who had not been adjudged to drink the cup, shall surely drink, And art thou, the one to go unpunished? Thou shalt not go unpunished, but shalt surely drink.
text.KJV: For thus saith the Lord; Behold, they whose judgment was not to drink of the cup have assuredly drunken; and art thou he that shall altogether go unpunished? thou shalt not go unpunished, but thou shalt surely drink of it.
text.NABRE: For thus says the Lord: Look, even those not sentenced to drink the cup must drink it! Shall you then go unpunished? You shall not! You shall drink every bit of it!
text.DRB: For thus saith our Lord: Behold they whose judgement was not to drink the cup, drinking shall drink: and shalt thou be left as innocent? Thou shalt not be innocent, but drinking thou shalt drink.

text.NRSV: For I have sworn by myself, says the Lord, that Bozrah shall become an object of horror and ridicule, a waste, and an object of cursing; and all her towns shall be perpetual wastes.
text.Rotherham: For by myself, have I sworn, Declareth Yahweh, That, Bozrah shall become, an astonishment: a reproach and a desolation, and a contempt,—And all her cities shall become age-abiding desolations.
text.KJV: For I have sworn by myself, saith the Lord, that Bozrah shall become a desolation, a reproach, a waste, and a curse; and all the cities thereof shall be perpetual wastes.
text.NABRE: By myself I have sworn—oracle of the Lord —Bozrah shall become an object of horror, a disgrace, a desolation, and a curse. Bozrah and all its cities shall become ruins forever.
text.DRB: Because I have sworn by myself, saith our Lord, that Bosra shall be into desolation, & into reproach, and into a desert, and into malediction: and all her cities shall be into everlasting desolations.

text.NRSV: I have heard tidings from the Lord, and a messenger has been sent among the nations: “Gather yourselves together and come against her, and rise up for battle!”
text.Rotherham: A report, have I heard from Yahweh, Yea, an envoy, throughout the nations, hath been sent,—Gather yourselves together and come against her, And arise to the battle.
text.KJV: I have heard a rumour from the Lord, and an ambassador is sent unto the heathen, saying, Gather ye together, and come against her, and rise up to the battle.
text.NABRE: I have heard a report from the Lord, a herald has been sent among the nations: “Gather together, move against it, get ready for battle!”
text.DRB: I have heard a bruit from our Lord, & a legate is sent to the Nations: Gather yourselves together, and come against her, and let us rise up into battle.

text.NRSV: For I will make you least among the nations, despised by humankind.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Small, have I made thee among the nations,—Despised among men!
text.KJV: For, lo, I will make thee small among the heathen, and despised among men.
text.NABRE: I will make you the least among the nations, despised by all people!
text.DRB: For behold I have made thee a little one in the Gentiles, contemptible among men.

text.NRSV: The terror you inspire and the pride of your heart have deceived you, you who live in the clefts of the rock, who hold the height of the hill. Although you make your nest as high as the eagle’s, from there I will bring you down, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thy monstrous thing, hath deceived thee, The insolence of thy heart, O thou that inhabitest the hidden recesses of the cliff, That holdest fast the height of the hill,—Though thou set high as an eagle, thy nest, From thence, will I bring thee down, Declareth Yahweh;
text.KJV: Thy terribleness hath deceived thee, and the pride of thine heart, O thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, that holdest the height of the hill: though thou shouldest make thy nest as high as the eagle, I will bring thee down from thence, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The terror you spread, the pride of your heart, beguiled you. You denizens of rocks and crevices, occupying towering peaks: Though you build your nest high as the eagle, from there I will bring you down—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: Thine arrogancy hath deceived thee, and the pride of thy heart: which dwellest in the caves of the rock, and endeavourest to apprehend the height of the hill. When thou shalt exalt thy nest as an eagle, thence will I bring thee down, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Edom shall become an object of horror; everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its disasters.
text.Rotherham: So shall, Edom, become an astonishment,—Every one passing by her, will be astonished and hiss, over all her plagues:
text.KJV: Also Edom shall be a desolation: every one that goeth by it shall be astonished, and shall hiss at all the plagues thereof.
text.NABRE: Edom shall become an object of horror. Passersby recoil in terror, hissing at all its wounds.
text.DRB: And Idumea shall be desolate: every one that shall pass by it, shall be astonished, & shall hiss upon all the plagues thereof.

text.NRSV: As when Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors were overthrown, says the Lord, no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in it.
text.Rotherham: Like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighbours, Saith Yahweh,—There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—any son of the earth-born.
text.KJV: As in the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighbour cities thereof, saith the Lord, no man shall abide there, neither shall a son of man dwell in it.
text.NABRE: As when Sodom, Gomorrah, and their neighbors were overthrown—oracle of the Lord —no one shall live in it, nor anyone settle there.
text.DRB: As Sodom is overthrown and Gomorrha, and her neighbours saith our Lord: there shall not a man dwell there, and there shall no son of man inhabit it.

text.NRSV: Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly chase Edom away from it; and I will appoint over it whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who can summon me? Who is the shepherd who can stand before me?
text.Rotherham: Lo! like a lion, will he come up from the majesty of the Jordan, unto the pasture perennial, But I will wink—I will make him run away therefrom, Who then shall be the Chosen One whom over it, I may set in charge? For who shall be like unto, me? And who shall appoint me, a time? And who is the Shepherd that shall stand before, me?
text.KJV: Behold, he shall come up like a lion from the swelling of Jordan against the habitation of the strong: but I will suddenly make him run away from her: and who is a chosen man, that I may appoint over her? for who is like me? and who will appoint me the time? and who is that shepherd that will stand before me?
text.NABRE: As when a lion comes up from a thicket of the Jordan to a permanent pasture, So in an instant, I will chase them off; I will establish there whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who holds me accountable? What shepherd can stand against me?
text.DRB: Behold as a lion he shall come up from the pride of Jordan, to the strong beautifulness: because I will make him run suddenly to her: & who shall be a chosen one whom I may appoint over her? For who is like to me? And who shall abide me? And who is this Pastor that can resist my countenance?

text.NRSV: Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Edom and the purposes he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman: Surely the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, hear ye The counsel of Yahweh which he hath counseled against Edom, And his devices which he hath devised against the inhabitants of Teman,—Surely the little ones of the flock shall drag them away, Surely he will cause their fold to be astounded over them.
text.KJV: Therefore hear the counsel of the Lord, that he hath taken against Edom; and his purposes, that he hath purposed against the inhabitants of Teman: Surely the least of the flock shall draw them out: surely he shall make their habitations desolate with them.
text.NABRE: Therefore, listen to the strategy the Lord devised for Edom; The plans he has drawn up against the inhabitants of Teman: They shall be dragged away, even the smallest of the flock; their pasture shall be aghast because of them.
text.DRB: Therefore hear ye the counsel of our Lord, which he hath taken concerning Edom: and his cogitations, which he hath thought concerning the inhabitants of Theman: If the little ones of the flock shall not cast them down, if they shall not destroy their habitation with them.

text.NRSV: At the sound of their fall the earth shall quake; the sound of their cry shall be heard at the Red Sea.
text.Rotherham: At the noise of their fall, hath trembled the earth, At the outcry, in the Red Sea, was heard its noise.
text.KJV: The earth is moved at the noise of their fall, at the cry the noise thereof was heard in the Red sea.
text.NABRE: With the din of their collapse the earth quakes, to the Red Sea the outcry is heard!
text.DRB: At the voice of their ruin the earth is moved: the cry of their voice is heard in the Red sea.

text.NRSV: Look, one shall mount up and swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Bozrah; and the heart of the warriors of Edom on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as an eagle, he shall mount and dart, and spread his wings over Bozrah,—So shall the heart of the heroes of Edom, in that day, become as the heart of a woman in her pain.
text.KJV: Behold, he shall come up and fly as the eagle, and spread his wings over Bozrah: and at that day shall the heart of the mighty men of Edom be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.
text.NABRE: Look! like an eagle he soars aloft, and spreads his wings over Bozrah; On that day the hearts of Edom’s warriors become like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.DRB: Behold he shall come up as an eagle, & fly out: and he shall spread his wings over Bosra: and the heart of the strong of Idumea shall be in that day, as the heart of a woman in travail.

text.NRSV: Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard bad news; they melt in fear, they are troubled like the sea that cannot be quiet.
text.Rotherham: Of Damascus. Turned pale have Hamath and Arpad, For, a calamitous report, have they heard—they tremble,—In the sea, is anxiety, it cannot, rest.
text.KJV: Concerning Damascus. Hamath is confounded, and Arpad: for they have heard evil tidings: they are fainthearted; there is sorrow on the sea; it cannot be quiet.
text.NABRE: Against Damascus. Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are shamed, for they have heard bad news; Anxious, they surge like the sea which cannot calm down.
text.DRB: To Damascus. Emath is confounded and Arphad: because they have heard a very ill bruit, they are troubled in the sea: for carefulness it could not be quiet.

text.NRSV: Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Enfeebled is Damascus She hath turned to flee But terror, hath seized her,—Anguish and pangs, have seized her as a woman in childbirth.
