text.DRB: Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon hath eaten me, he hath devoured me: he hath made me as an empty vessel: he hath swallowed me up as a dragon, he hath filled his belly with my tenderness, and hath cast me out.

text.NRSV: May my torn flesh be upon Babylon,” the inhabitant of Zion shall say. “May my blood be upon the inhabitants of Chaldea,” Jerusalem shall say.
text.Rotherham: The violence done to me and to my flesh, be upon Babylon! Shall the inhabitress of Zion say,—Yea, my blood, be upon the inhabitant of Chaldea! Shall Jerusalem say.
text.KJV: The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon, shall the inhabitant of Zion say; and my blood upon the inhabitants of Chaldea, shall Jerusalem say.
text.NABRE: Let my torn flesh be visited upon Babylon,” says enthroned Zion; “My blood upon the inhabitants of Chaldea,” says Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Iniquity against me, and my flesh upon Babylon, saith the habitation of Sion: and my blood upon the inhabitants of Chaldee, saith Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord: I am going to plead your cause and take vengeance for you. I will dry up her sea and make her fountain dry;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh, Behold me! pleading thy cause, So then I will execute the avenging of thee; And will dry up her sea, And make dry her spring:
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord; Behold, I will plead thy cause, and take vengeance for thee; and I will dry up her sea, and make her springs dry.
text.NABRE: But now, thus says the Lord: I will certainly defend your cause, I will certainly avenge you; I will dry up her sea, and drain her fountain.
text.DRB: Therefore thus saith our Lord: Behold I will judge thy cause, and will revenge thy vengeance, & I will make her sea desolate, & will dry up her vein.

text.NRSV: and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins, a den of jackals, an object of horror and of hissing, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Babylon become—Heaps A habitation of jackals An astonishment, and A hissing, Without inhabitant.
text.KJV: And Babylon shall become heaps, a dwellingplace for dragons, an astonishment, and an hissing, without an inhabitant.
text.NABRE: Babylon shall become a heap of ruins, a haunt of jackals; A place of horror and hissing, without inhabitants.
text.DRB: And Babylon shall be into heaps, the habitation of dragons, astonishment, and hissing, because there is not an inhabiter.

text.NRSV: They shall roar together like lions; they shall growl like lions’ whelps.
text.Rotherham: Together, like wild lions, shall they roar,—They have growled like lions’ whelps.
text.KJV: They shall roar together like lions: they shall yell as lions’ whelps.
text.NABRE: They roar like lions, growl like lion cubs.
text.DRB: They shall roar together as lions, they shall shake the manes as the whelps of lions,

text.NRSV: When they are inflamed, I will set them a feast and make them drunk, until they become merry and then sleep a perpetual sleep and never awake, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: When they are heated, I will spread their banquets, And let them drink that they may become uproarious, So shall they sleep an age-abiding sleep and not wake,—Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: In their heat I will make their feasts, and I will make them drunken, that they may rejoice, and sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: When they are parched, I will set drink before them to make them drunk, that they may be overcome with everlasting sleep, never to awaken— oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: In their heat I will set their drink: and I will make them drunk, that they may be drowsy, and sleep an everlasting sleep, and not arise, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams and goats.
text.Rotherham: I will bring them down As fat lambs to slaughter,—As rams with he-goats.
text.KJV: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with he goats.
text.NABRE: I will bring them down like lambs to slaughter, like rams and goats.
text.DRB: I will lead them as lambs to be a victim, and as rams with kids.

text.NRSV: “How Sheshach is taken, the pride of the whole earth seized! How Babylon has become an object of horror among the nations!
text.Rotherham: How hath Sheshach, been captured! How hath the praise of all the earth been seized! How hath Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!
text.KJV: How is Sheshach taken! and how is the praise of the whole earth surprised! how is Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!
text.NABRE: How she has been seized, taken captive, the glory of the whole world! What a horror Babylon has become among the nations:
text.DRB: How is Sesach taken, and the noble one of all the earth apprehended? How is Babylon become an astonishment among the Gentiles?

text.NRSV: The sea has risen over Babylon; she has been covered by its roaring waves.
text.Rotherham: The sea, hath gone up over Babylon,—With the multitude of its rolling waves, is she covered.
text.KJV: The sea is come up upon Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of the waves thereof.
text.NABRE: against Babylon the sea rises, she is overwhelmed by roaring waves!
text.DRB: The sea is come up over Babylon: she is covered with the multitude of the waves thereof.

text.NRSV: Her towns have become a desolation, a dry land and a desert, a land in which no one lives, and through which no mortal passes.
text.Rotherham: Her cities have become, an astonishment, A land parched up, and a waste plain,—A land wherein shall no man dwell, Nor pass through them a son of the earth-born!
text.KJV: Her cities are a desolation, a dry land, and a wilderness, a land wherein no man dwelleth, neither doth any son of man pass thereby.
text.NABRE: Her cities have become wasteland, a parched and arid land Where no one lives, no one passes through.
text.DRB: Her cities are become an astonishment: a land inhabitable and desolate, a land wherein none can dwell, nor son of man may pass by it.

text.NRSV: I will punish Bel in Babylon, and make him disgorge what he has swallowed. The nations shall no longer stream to him; the wall of Babylon has fallen.
text.Rotherham: So will I bring punishment upon Bel in Babylon, And will bring forth what he hath swallowed out of his mouth, And the nations shall stream unto him no more,—Even the wall of Babylon hath fallen!
text.KJV: And I will punish Bel in Babylon, and I will bring forth out of his mouth that which he hath swallowed up: and the nations shall not flow together any more unto him: yea, the wall of Babylon shall fall.
text.NABRE: I will punish Bel in Babylon, and make him vomit up what he swallowed; nations shall no longer stream to him. Even the wall of Babylon falls!
text.DRB: And I will visit upon Bel in Babylon, and I will cast out of his mouth that which he had swallowed: and the Gentiles shall no more run together unto him, for the wall also of Babylon shall fall.

text.NRSV: “Come out of her, my people! Save your lives, each of you, from the fierce anger of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Come ye forth out of her midst O my people, And deliver ye every man his own life,—Because of the glow of the anger of Yahweh.
text.KJV: My people, go ye out of the midst of her, and deliver ye every man his soul from the fierce anger of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Leave her, my people; each of you save your own life from the burning wrath of the Lord.
text.DRB: Go out of the midst of her my people: that every one may save his life from the wrath of the fury of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Do not be fainthearted or fearful at the rumors heard in the land—one rumor comes one year and another the next, a rumor of violence in the land and of ruler against ruler.
text.Rotherham: And let not your heart be timid, nor be ye afraid, Because of the report that is reported in the earth When there shall come in one year, the report And after that in another year, the report, With violence in the earth, and ruler upon ruler.
text.KJV: And lest your heart faint, and ye fear for the rumour that shall be heard in the land; a rumour shall both come one year, and after that in another year shall come a rumour, and violence in the land, ruler against ruler.
text.NABRE: Do not be discouraged when rumors spread through the land; this year one rumor comes, next year another: “Violence in the land!” or “Ruler against ruler!”
text.DRB: And lest perhaps your hearts faint, and ye fear the bruit, that shall be heard in the land: and there shall come a bruit in the year, & after this year a bruit: and iniquity in the land, & ruler upon ruler.

text.NRSV: “Therefore the days are surely coming when I will punish the images of Babylon; her whole land shall be put to shame, and all her slain shall fall in her midst.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days coming when I will bring punishment upon the images of Babylon, And all her land, shall turn pale,—Yea, all her wounded, shall fall in her midst.
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, the days come, that I will do judgment upon the graven images of Babylon: and her whole land shall be confounded, and all her slain shall fall in the midst of her.
text.NABRE: Realize that the days are coming when I will punish the idols of Babylon; the whole land shall be put to shame, all her slain shall fall in her midst.
text.DRB: Therefore behold the days come, and I will visit upon the sculptiles of Babylon: and all her land shall be confounded and all her slain shall fall in the midst of her.

text.NRSV: Then the heavens and the earth, and all that is in them, shall shout for joy over Babylon; for the destroyers shall come against her out of the north, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall shout over Babylon—heavens and earth and all who are therein,—For out of the North, shall come to her the spoilers Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then the heaven and the earth, and all that is therein, shall sing for Babylon: for the spoilers shall come unto her from the north, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then heaven and earth and everything in them shall shout over Babylon with joy, when the destroyers come against her from the north—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the heavens and the earth shall praise upon Babylon, & all things that are in them: because spoilers shall come to her from the North, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Babylon must fall for the slain of Israel, as the slain of all the earth have fallen because of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Not only hath Babylon, caused the fall of the slain of Israel,—By Babylon, also have fallen the slain of all the earth.
text.KJV: As Babylon hath caused the slain of Israel to fall, so at Babylon shall fall the slain of all the earth.
text.NABRE: Babylon, too, must fall, you slain of Israel, because by the hand of Babylon the slain of all the earth have fallen.
text.DRB: And as Babylon caused that there should fall slain in Israel: so of Babylon there shall fall slain in the whole land.

text.NRSV: “You who have escaped from the sword, go, do not linger! Remember the Lord from far away, and let Jerusalem come into your mind:
text.Rotherham: Ye that have escaped the sword, depart do not stand still,—Remember, from afar Yahweh, Let, Jerusalem, come up on your heart:-
text.KJV: Ye that have escaped the sword, go away, stand not still: remember the Lord afar off, and let Jerusalem come into your mind.
text.NABRE: You who have escaped the sword, go, do not stand idle; Remember the Lord from far away, let Jerusalem come to mind.
text.DRB: You that have escaped the sword, come, stand not: remember our Lord a far off, and let Jerusalem ascend upon your heart.

text.NRSV: “We are put to shame, for we have heard insults; dishonor has covered our faces, for strangers have come into the holy places of the Lord’s house.”
text.Rotherham: We have turned pale for we have heard a reproach, Confusion, hath covered our faces,—For aliens have entered upon the hallowed places of the house of Yahweh!
text.KJV: We are confounded, because we have heard reproach: shame hath covered our faces: for strangers are come into the sanctuaries of the Lord’s house.
text.NABRE: We are ashamed because we have heard taunts, disgrace covers our faces; strangers have entered sanctuaries in the Lord ’s house.
text.DRB: We are confounded, because we have heard reproach: ignominy hath covered our faces: because strangers are come upon the sanctification of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: “Therefore the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will punish her images, and through all her land the wounded shall groan.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When I will bring punishment upon her images; And throughout all her land, shall the pierced one groan.
text.KJV: Wherefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will do judgment upon her graven images: and through all her land the wounded shall groan.
text.NABRE: Therefore see, the days are coming—oracle of the Lord — when I will punish her idols, and throughout the land the wounded will groan.
text.DRB: Therefore behold the days come, saith our Lord: & I will visit upon her sculptiles, and in all her land the wounded shall roar.

text.NRSV: Though Babylon should mount up to heaven, and though she should fortify her strong height, from me destroyers would come upon her, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Though Babylon should mount the heavens, And though she should fortify her strong high-place, From me, should come spoilers unto her Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Though Babylon should mount up to heaven, and though she should fortify the height of her strength, yet from me shall spoilers come unto her, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Though Babylon scale the heavens, and make her strong heights inaccessible, my destroyers shall reach her—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: If Babylon shall ascend up into heaven, and stablish her strength on high, from me there shall come wasters of her, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: “Listen! —a cry from Babylon! A great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!
text.Rotherham: A voice of outcry from Babylon! And a great crash from the land of the Chaldeans!
text.KJV: A sound of a cry cometh from Babylon, and great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans:
text.NABRE: A sound of crying from Babylon, great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans;
text.DRB: Avoice of crying from Babylon, and great destruction from the Land of the Chaldees:

text.NRSV: For the Lord is laying Babylon waste, and stilling her loud voice. Their waves roar like mighty waters, the noise of their voice is raised;
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, is spoiling Babylon, And will destroy out of her the loud voice,—Though their waves have roared like many waters, Been uttered the loud boast of their voice.
text.KJV: Because the Lord hath spoiled Babylon, and destroyed out of her the great voice; when her waves do roar like great waters, a noise of their voice is uttered:
text.NABRE: For the Lord lays Babylon waste, silences her loud cry, Waves roaring like mighty waters, a clamor resounding.
text.DRB: because our Lord hath wasted Babylon, and destroyed out of it the great voice: & their waves shall sound as many waters: their voice hath given a sound.

text.NRSV: for a destroyer has come against her, against Babylon; her warriors are taken, their bows are broken; for the Lord is a God of recompense, he will surely repay.
text.Rotherham: For there hath come upon her—upon Babylon—a spoiler, And captured are their heroes And broken are their bows,—For, a GOD of recompenses, is Yahweh, He will surely repay.
text.KJV: Because the spoiler is come upon her, even upon Babylon, and her mighty men are taken, every one of their bows is broken: for the Lord God of recompences shall surely requite.
text.NABRE: For the destroyer comes upon her, upon Babylon; warriors are captured, their bows broken; The Lord is a God of recompense, he will surely repay.
text.DRB: Because the spoiler is come upon her, that is, upon Babylon, & her valiants are apprehended, & their bow is weakened: because the strong revenger our Lord rendering will repay.

text.NRSV: I will make her officials and her sages drunk, also her governors, her deputies, and her warriors; they shall sleep a perpetual sleep and never awake, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make drunk—Her princes and her wise men, Her governors, and her deputies and her heroes, And they shall sleep an age-abiding sleep, and not wake,—Declareth the King, Yahweh of hosts, is his name.
text.KJV: And I will make drunk her princes, and her wise men, her captains, and her rulers, and her mighty men: and they shall sleep a perpetual sleep, and not wake, saith the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.NABRE: I will make her princes and sages drunk, with her governors, officers, and warriors, so that they sleep an everlasting sleep, never to awaken—oracle of the King, whose name is Lord of hosts.
text.DRB: And I will inebriate her Princes, & her wise men, & her Dukes, and her magistrates, and her valiants: and they shall sleep an everlasting sleep, and shall not awake, saith the King, the Lord of hosts is his name.

text.NRSV: “Thus says the Lord of hosts: The broad wall of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground, and her high gates shall be burned with fire. The peoples exhaust themselves for nothing, and the nations weary themselves only for fire.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts—The broad walls of Babylon, shall be laid utterly bare, And, her lofty gates, with fire, shall be burned,—And peoples shall labour for emptiness, And populations, for the fire, shall weary themselves.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord of hosts; The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly broken, and her high gates shall be burned with fire; and the people shall labour in vain, and the folk in the fire, and they shall be weary.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord of hosts: The walls of spacious Babylon shall be leveled to the ground, its lofty gates destroyed by fire. The toil of the peoples is for nothing; the nations weary themselves for what the flames consume.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord of hosts: That most broad wall of Babylon by undermining shall be undermined, and her high gates shall be burnt with fire, and the labours of the peoples shall come to nothing, and of the nations shall be into the fire, & shall perish.

text.NRSV: The word that the prophet Jeremiah commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah, when he went with King Zedekiah of Judah to Babylon, in the fourth year of his reign. Seraiah was the quartermaster.
text.Rotherham: The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah, when he went with Zedekiah king of Judah into Babylon, in the fourth year of his reign,—now Seraiah, was travelling-marshal.
text.KJV: The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah, the son of Maaseiah, when he went with Zedekiah the king of Judah into Babylon in the fourth year of his reign. And this Seraiah was a quiet prince.
text.NABRE: The Prophecy Sent to Babylon. The mission Jeremiah the prophet gave to Seraiah, son of Neriah, son of Mahseiah, when he went to Babylon with King Zedekiah, king of Judah, in the fourth year of his reign; Seraiah was chief quartermaster.
text.DRB: The word that Jeremy the Prophet commanded Saraias the son of Nerias, the son of Maasias, when he went with Sedecias the king into Babylon, in the fourth year of his kingdom: and Saraias was the Prince of Prophecy.

text.NRSV: Jeremiah wrote in a scroll all the disasters that would come on Babylon, all these words that are written concerning Babylon.
text.Rotherham: So Jeremiah wrote all the calamity which was to come unto Babylon in one scroll; even all these words which have been written against Babylon.
text.KJV: So Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon, even all these words that are written against Babylon.
text.NABRE: Jeremiah wrote down on one scroll the disaster that would befall Babylon; all these words were written against Babylon.
text.DRB: And Jeremy wrote all the evil, that was to come upon Babylon in one Book: all these words, that are written against Babylon.

text.NRSV: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah: “When you come to Babylon, see that you read all these words,
text.Rotherham: Then said Jeremiah unto Seraiah,—When thou comest into Babylon, then shalt thou look out and read all these words;
text.KJV: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah, When thou comest to Babylon, and shalt see, and shalt read all these words;
text.NABRE: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah: “When you reach Babylon, see that you read all these words aloud,
text.DRB: And Jeremy said to Saraias: When thou shalt come into Babylon, and shalt see, & shalt read all these words,

text.NRSV: and say, ‘O Lord, you yourself threatened to destroy this place so that neither human beings nor animals shall live in it, and it shall be desolate forever.’
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say—O Yahweh! thou, thyself, hast spoken against this place to cut it off, That there be in it no inhabitant Neither man nor beast,—But desolations age-abiding, shall it become!
text.KJV: Then shalt thou say, O Lord, thou hast spoken against this place, to cut it off, that none shall remain in it, neither man nor beast, but that it shall be desolate for ever.
text.NABRE: and then say: Lord, you yourself spoke against this place in order to cut it down so that nothing, human being or beast, could live in it, because it is to remain a wasteland forever.
text.DRB: thou shalt say: Lord thou hast spoken against this place to destroy it: that there be none to inhabit it from man even unto beast, and that it be a perpetual wilderness.

text.NRSV: When you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it, and throw it into the middle of the Euphrates,
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when thou hast made an end of reading this scroll, that thou shalt bind thereunto a stone, and cast it in the midst of the Euphrates,
text.KJV: And it shall be, when thou hast made an end of reading this book, that thou shalt bind a stone to it, and cast it into the midst of Euphrates:
text.NABRE: When you have finished reading this scroll, tie a stone to it and throw it into the Euphrates,
text.DRB: And when thou shalt have finished reading this Book, thou shalt tie a stone to it, and shalt throw it into the midst of Euphrates:

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus shall Babylon sink, to rise no more, because of the disasters that I am bringing on her.’ ” Thus far are the words of Jeremiah.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say,—In like manner, shall Babylon sink and not rise Because of the calamity which I am about to bring thereupon: So shall they perish. Thus far, are, the words of Jeremiah.
text.KJV: And thou shalt say, Thus shall Babylon sink, and shall not rise from the evil that I will bring upon her: and they shall be weary. Thus far are the words of Jeremiah.
text.NABRE: and say: Thus Babylon shall sink. It will never rise, because of the disaster I am bringing upon it.” Thus far the words of Jeremiah.
text.DRB: and thou shalt say: So shall Babylon be drowned, and she shall not rise up from the face of the affliction, that I will bring upon her, and she shall be dissolved. Hitherto the words of Jeremy.

text.NRSV: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-one years old, was Zedekiah when he began to reign, and eleven years, reigned he, in Jerusalem,—and, his mother’s name, was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
text.KJV: Zedekiah was one and twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
text.NABRE: IX. Historical Appendix Chapter 52 Capture of Jerusalem. Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah from Libnah.
text.DRB: A child of one and twenty years was Sedecias when he began to reign: and eleven years he reigned in Jerusalem: and the name of his mother was Amital, the daughter of Jeremie of Lobna.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, just as Jehoiakim had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that Jehoiakim had done.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the eyes of the Lord, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, just as Jehoiakim had done.
text.DRB: And he did evil in the eyes of our Lord, according to all things that Joakim had done.

text.NRSV: Indeed, Jerusalem and Judah so angered the Lord that he expelled them from his presence. Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: For it was, because the anger of Yahweh, had come against Jerusalem and Judah until he had cast them out from his presence, that Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
text.KJV: For through the anger of the Lord it came to pass in Jerusalem and Judah, till he had cast them out from his presence, that Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
text.NABRE: Indeed, the things done in Jerusalem and in Judah so angered the Lord that he cast them out from his presence. Thus Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
text.DRB: Because the fury of our Lord was against Jerusalem, & against Juda, till he cast them away from his face: and Sedecias revolted from the king of Babylon.

text.NRSV: And in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon came with all his army against Jerusalem, and they laid siege to it; they built siegeworks against it all around.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth of the month, that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his force, against Jerusalem, and encamped against it,—and he built against it a siege-wall round about.
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, in the tenth day of the month, that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his army, against Jerusalem, and pitched against it, and built forts against it round about.
text.NABRE: In the tenth month of the ninth year of his reign, on the tenth day of the month, Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, and his entire army advanced against Jerusalem, encamped around it, and built siege walls on every side.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, the tenth of the month, came Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon, himself and all his army against Jerusalem, and they besieged it, and built against it munitions round about.

text.NRSV: So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
text.Rotherham: And the city came into the siege,—until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
text.KJV: So the city was besieged unto the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
text.NABRE: The siege of the city continued until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
text.DRB: And the city was besieged until the eleventh year of king Sedecias.

text.NRSV: On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine became so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: In the fourth month on the ninth of the month, when the famine had become severe in the city,—and there had come to be no bread for the people of the land,
text.KJV: And in the fourth month, in the ninth day of the month, the famine was sore in the city, so that there was no bread for the people of the land.
text.NABRE: On the ninth day of the fourth month, when famine had gripped the city and the people had no more bread,
text.DRB: And in the fourth month, the ninth of the month, a famine possessed the city: and there were no victuals for the people of the land.

text.NRSV: Then a breach was made in the city wall; and all the soldiers fled and went out from the city by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, by the king’s garden, though the Chaldeans were all around the city. They went in the direction of the Arabah.
text.Rotherham: then was the city broken up, and all the men of war, beginning to flee went forth out of the city by night, by way of the gate between the two walls which was by the garden of the king ()the Chaldeans being near the city round about),—and they went the way towards the Waste Plain.
text.KJV: Then the city was broken up, and all the men of war fled, and went forth out of the city by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, which was by the king’s garden; (now the Chaldeans were by the city round about:) and they went by the way of the plain.
text.NABRE: the city walls were breached. All the soldiers fled and left the city by night through the gate between the two walls which was near the king’s garden. With the Chaldeans surrounding the city, they went in the direction of the Arabah.
text.DRB: And a breach was made into the city, and all the men of war fled, and went out of the city in the night by the way of the gate that is between the two walls, and leadeth to the king's garden (the Chaldees besieging the city round about) & they departed by the way, that leadeth into the wilderness.

text.NRSV: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered, deserting him.
text.Rotherham: And the force of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and overtook Zedekiah, in the Waste Plains of Jericho,—and, all his force, was scattered from him.
text.KJV: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered from him.
text.NABRE: But the Chaldean army pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the wilderness near Jericho; his whole army fled from him.
text.DRB: But the army of the Chaldees pursued the king: and they apprehended Sedecias in the desert, which is beside Jericho: and all his train fled scattering from him.

text.NRSV: Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and he passed sentence on him.
text.Rotherham: So they seized the king, and brought him up, unto the king of Babylon at Riblah, in the land of Hamath,—and he pronounced upon him sentences of judgment.
text.KJV: Then they took the king, and carried him up unto the king of Babylon to Riblah in the land of Hamath; where he gave judgment upon him.
text.NABRE: The king, therefore, was arrested and brought to Riblah, in the land of Hamath, to the king of Babylon, who pronounced judgment on him.
text.DRB: And when they had taken the king, they brought him to the king of Babylon into Reblatha, which is in the land of Emath; and he spake to him judgements.

text.NRSV: The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and also slaughtered all the officers of Judah at Riblah.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes,—moreover also all the princes of Judah, slew he in Riblah;
text.KJV: And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes: he slew also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.
text.NABRE: As Zedekiah looked on, the king of Babylon slaughtered his sons before his eyes! All the nobles of Judah were slaughtered at Riblah.
text.DRB: And the king of Babylon killed the sons of Sedecias before his eyes: yea & all the Princes of Juda he slew in Reblatha.

text.NRSV: He put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him in fetters of bronze; the king of Babylon took him to Babylon and put him in prison until the day of his death.
text.Rotherham: and the eyes of Zedekiah, put he out,—and bound him with fetters of bronze and the king of Babylon took him to Babylon, and put him in prison—until the day of his death.
text.KJV: Then he put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him in chains, and carried him to Babylon, and put him in prison till the day of his death.
text.NABRE: And the eyes of Zedekiah he then blinded, bound him with chains, and the king of Babylon brought him to Babylon and kept him in prison until the day he died.
text.DRB: And he plucked out the eyes of Sedecias, & bound him with fetters, & the king of Babylon brought him into Babylon: and he put him in the prison house even to the day of his death.

text.NRSV: In the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month—which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon—Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard who served the king of Babylon, entered Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And in the fifth month on the tenth of the month, the same, was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon, came Nebuzaradan, chief of the royal executioners,—who stood before the king of Babylon into, Jerusalem;
text.KJV: Now in the fifth month, in the tenth day of the month, which was the nineteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon, came Nebuzar–adan, captain of the guard, which served the king of Babylon, into Jerusalem,
text.NABRE: Destruction of Jerusalem. On the tenth day of the fifth month, this was in the nineteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar, king of Babylon, Nebuzaradan, captain of the bodyguard, came to Jerusalem as the representative of the king of Babylon.
text.DRB: And in the fifth month, the tenth of the month, the same is the nineteenth year of Nabuchodonosor the king of Babylon: came Nabuzardan the Prince of the warfare, who stood before the king of Babylon in Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He burned the house of the Lord, the king’s house, and all the houses of Jerusalem; every great house he burned down.
text.Rotherham: and he burned the house of Yahweh and the house of the king,—yea all the houses of Jerusalem even every great man’s house, burned he with fire;
text.KJV: And burned the house of the Lord, and the king’s house; and all the houses of Jerusalem, and all the houses of the great men, burned he with fire:
text.NABRE: He burned the house of the Lord, the palace of the king, and all the houses of Jerusalem; every large building he destroyed with fire.
text.DRB: And he burnt the house of our Lord. And the king's house, and all the houses of Jerusalem, and every great house he burnt with fire.

text.NRSV: All the army of the Chaldeans, who were with the captain of the guard, broke down all the walls around Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and all the walls of Jerusalem round about, did all the force of the Chaldeans who were with the chief of the royal executioners, break down.
text.KJV: And all the army of the Chaldeans, that were with the captain of the guard, brake down all the walls of Jerusalem round about.
text.NABRE: Then the Chaldean troops with the captain of the guard tore down all the walls that surrounded Jerusalem.
text.DRB: And all the host of the Chaldees that was with the Prince of the warfare, destroyed all the wall of Jerusalem round about.

text.NRSV: Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried into exile some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had defected to the king of Babylon, together with the rest of the artisans.
text.Rotherham: And some of the poor of the people and the residue of the people who were left in the city, and the disheartened who had fallen away unto the king of Babylon and the residue of the multitude, did Nebuzaradan chief of the royal executioners carry away captive.
text.KJV: Then Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard carried away captive certain of the poor of the people, and the residue of the people that remained in the city, and those that fell away, that fell to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude.
text.NABRE: Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard, led into exile the remnant of people left in the city, those who had deserted to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the artisans.
text.DRB: But of the poor of the people, and of the rest of the vulgar sort, which remained in the city, and of the fugitives, that were fled to the king of Babylon, and the rest of the multitude, Nabuzardan the Prince of the warfare transported.

text.NRSV: But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest people of the land to be vinedressers and tillers of the soil.
text.Rotherham: But, others of the poor of the land, did Nebuzaradan chief of the royal executioners leave, for vinedressers and for husbandmen.
text.KJV: But Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard left certain of the poor of the land for vinedressers and for husbandmen.
text.NABRE: But Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard, left behind some of the country’s poor as vinedressers and farmers.
text.DRB: But of the poor of the land Nabuzardan the prince of the warfare left some to be dressers of vineyards, and husbandmen.

text.NRSV: The bronze pillars that were in the house of the Lord, and the stands and the bronze sea that were in the house of the Lord, the Chaldeans broke in pieces, and carried all the bronze to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: And the pillars of bronze that pertained to the house of Yahweh and the stands and the sea of bronze which was in the house of Yahweh, did the Chaldeans, break in pieces,—and they carried away all the bronze of them to Babylon;
text.KJV: Also the pillars of brass that were in the house of the Lord, and the bases, and the brasen sea that was in the house of the Lord, the Chaldeans brake, and carried all the brass of them to Babylon.
text.NABRE: The bronze pillars that belonged to the house of the Lord, and the wheeled carts and the bronze sea in the house of the Lord, the Chaldeans broke into pieces; they carried away all the bronze to Babylon.
text.DRB: The brazen pillars also, that were in the house of our Lord, and the feet, and the sea of brass, that was in the house of our Lord, the Chaldees brake: and they took all the brass of them into Babylon.

text.NRSV: They took the pots, the shovels, the snuffers, the basins, the ladles, and all the bronze vessels used in the temple service.
text.Rotherham: and the caldrons and the shovels and the snuffers and the dashing bowls, and the spoons even all the utensils of bronze wherewith ministration used to be made did they take away;
text.KJV: The caldrons also, and the shovels, and the snuffers, and the bowls, and the spoons, and all the vessels of brass wherewith they ministered, took they away.
text.NABRE: They also took the pots, shovels, snuffers, bowls, pans, and all the bronze vessels used for service;
text.DRB: And the kettles, and the fleshhooks, and the psalteries, and the phials, and the little mortars, and all the brazen vessels, that had been in the ministry, they took:

text.NRSV: The captain of the guard took the bowls, the firepans, the basins, the pots, the lampstands, the ladles, and the cups, all that were made of gold and all that were made of silver.
text.Rotherham: and the basins and the censers and the dashing bowls and the caldrons and the lamps and the spoons and the cups, which were of gold in gold, and which were of silver in silver, did the chief of the royal executioners, take away.
text.KJV: And the basons, and the firepans, and the bowls, and the caldrons, and the candlesticks, and the spoons, and the cups; that which was of gold in gold, and that which was of silver in silver, took the captain of the guard away.
text.NABRE: the basins, fire holders, bowls, pots, lampstands, pans, the sacrificial bowls made of gold or silver. Along with these furnishings the captain of the guard carried off
text.DRB: and the water pots, and the censers, and the pitchers, and the basins, and the candlesticks, & the mortars, & the goblets: as many as of gold, of gold, & as many as of silver, of silver, did the Prince of the warfare take:

text.NRSV: As for the two pillars, the one sea, the twelve bronze bulls that were under the sea, and the stands, which King Solomon had made for the house of the Lord, the bronze of all these vessels was beyond weighing.
text.Rotherham: As for the two pillars the one sea, and the twelve oxen of bronze which were under the stands which King Solomon had made for the house of Yahweh, without weight was the bronze of all these things.
text.KJV: The two pillars, one sea, and twelve brasen bulls that were under the bases, which king Solomon had made in the house of the Lord: the brass of all these vessels was without weight.
text.NABRE: the two pillars, the one sea and its base of twelve oxen cast in bronze, and the wheeled carts King Solomon had commissioned for the house of the Lord. The bronze from all these furnishings was impossible to weigh.
text.DRB: and two pillars, & one sea, & twelve oxen of brass, that were under the feet, which king Salomon had made in the house of our Lord: there was no weight of the brass of all these vessels.

text.NRSV: As for the pillars, the height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, its circumference was twelve cubits; it was hollow and its thickness was four fingers.
text.Rotherham: Now as for the pillars, eighteen cubits, was the height of each pillar, and, a line of twelve cubits, compassed it about,—and the thickness thereof was four fingers’ breadth—hollow;
text.KJV: And concerning the pillars, the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits; and a fillet of twelve cubits did compass it; and the thickness thereof was four fingers: it was hollow.
text.NABRE: As for the pillars, each of them was eighteen cubits high and twelve cubits in diameter; each was four fingers thick and hollow inside.
text.DRB: And concerning the pillars, there were eighteen cubits of height in one pillar: & a cord of twelve cubits did compass it about: moreover the thickness thereof, of four fingers, and within it was hollow.

text.NRSV: Upon it was a capital of bronze; the height of the one capital was five cubits; a network and pomegranates, all of bronze, were on the capital all around. And the second pillar had the same, with pomegranates.
text.Rotherham: and there was, a capital, upon it, of bronze, and the height of each capital, was five cubits, with lattice-work and pomegranates upon the capital round about—the whole was of bronze,—and like these, were the second pillar and the pomegranates.
text.KJV: And a chapiter of brass was upon it; and the height of one chapiter was five cubits, with network and pomegranates upon the chapiters round about, all of brass. The second pillar also and the pomegranates were like unto these.
text.NABRE: A bronze capital five cubits high crowned the one pillar, and a network with pomegranates encircled the capital, all of bronze; and so for the other pillar, with pomegranates.
text.DRB: And the little heads of brass upon both: the height of one little head, of five cubits: and the little nets, and the pomegranates upon the crown round about, all of brass. Likewise of the second pillar, & the pomegranates.

text.NRSV: There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates surrounding the network were one hundred.
text.Rotherham: And the pomegranates were ninety-six on a side,—all the pomegranates were a hundred upon the lattice-work round about.
text.KJV: And there were ninety and six pomegranates on a side; and all the pomegranates upon the network were an hundred round about.
text.NABRE: There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides, a hundred pomegranates surrounding the network.
text.DRB: And there were ninety six pomegranates hanging down: and all the pomegranates an hundred, were compassed with little nets.

text.NRSV: The captain of the guard took the chief priest Seraiah, the second priest Zephaniah, and the three guardians of the threshold;
text.Rotherham: And the chief of the royal executioners, took away, Seraiah the first priest, and Zephaniah, the second priest,—and the three keepers of the entrance-hall;
text.KJV: And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest, and Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the door:
text.NABRE: The captain of the guard also took Seraiah the high priest, Zephaniah the second priest, and the three keepers of the entrance.
text.DRB: And the master of the warfare took Saraias the chief Priest, and Sophonias the second Priest: and the three keepers of the entry.

text.NRSV: and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the soldiers, and seven men of the king’s council who were found in the city; the secretary of the commander of the army who mustered the people of the land; and sixty men of the people of the land who were found inside the city.
text.Rotherham: and out of the city, took he one eunuch who was in charge over the men of war and seven men of them who used to watch the face of the king, who were found in the city, and the scribe of the prince of the host, who used to muster the people of the land,—and sixty men of the people of the land, who were found in the midst of the city.
text.KJV: He took also out of the city an eunuch, which had the charge of the men of war; and seven men of them that were near the king’s person, which were found in the city; and the principal scribe of the host, who mustered the people of the land; and threescore men of the people of the land, that were found in the midst of the city.
text.NABRE: From the city he took one courtier, a commander of soldiers, and seven men in the personal service of the king still in the city, the scribe of the army commander who mustered the people of the land, and sixty of the common people remaining in the city.
text.DRB: And of the city he took one eunuch, that was chief over the men of war: and seven men of them, that saw the king's face, that were found in the city: and a scribe the captain of the soldiers, who tried the young soldiers: and three score of the people of the land, that were found in the midst of the city.

text.NRSV: Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
text.Rotherham: And when Nebuzaradan chief the royal executioners, had taken them, and brought them unto the king of Babylon at Riblah,
text.KJV: So Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard took them, and brought them to the king of Babylon to Riblah.
text.NABRE: The captain of the guard, Nebuzaradan, arrested them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah,
text.DRB: And Nabuzardan the Prince of the warfare took them, and he brought them to the king of Babylon into Reblatha.

text.NRSV: And the king of Babylon struck them down, and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah went into exile out of its land.
text.Rotherham: then did the king of Babylon smite them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath,—thus carried he Judah captive away from off their own soil.
text.KJV: And the king of Babylon smote them, and put them to death in Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of his own land.
text.NABRE: who had them struck down and executed in Riblah, in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was exiled from the land.
text.DRB: And the king of Babylon struck them: and he killed them in Reblatha in the land of Emath: and Juda was transported from his land.

text.NRSV: This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadrezzar exiled: in the seventh year, three thousand twenty-three Judeans;
text.Rotherham: This, is the people, whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive,—In the seventh year—of them of Judah, three thousand and twenty-three;
text.KJV: This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand Jews and three and twenty:
text.NABRE: This is the number of people Nebuchadnezzar led away captive: in his seventh year, three thousand twenty-three people of Judah;
text.DRB: This is the people, which Nabuchodonosor transported: In the seventh year, Jews three thousand and twenty three.

text.NRSV: in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he exiled from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty-two persons;
text.Rotherham: In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar—out of Jerusalem, eight hundred and thirty-two souls;
text.KJV: In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty and two persons:
text.NABRE: in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar, eight hundred thirty-two persons from Jerusalem;
text.DRB: In the eighteenth year of Nabuchodonosor from Jerusalem souls eight hundred thirty two.

text.NRSV: in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard exiled of the Judeans seven hundred forty-five persons; all the persons were four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: In the three-and-twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar, Nebuzaradan, chief of the royal executioners took away captive, of them of Judah, seven hundred and forty-five souls: All, the souls, were four thousand and six hundred.
text.KJV: In the three and twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar Nebuzar–adan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the Jews seven hundred forty and five persons: all the persons were four thousand and six hundred.
text.NABRE: in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadnezzar, Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard, deported seven hundred forty-five Judahites: four thousand six hundred persons in all. Favor Shown to Jehoiachin.
text.DRB: In the three and twentieth year of Nabuchodonosor, Nabuzardan the Prince of the warfare transported of the Jews seven hundred forty five souls. All the souls therefore were four thousand six hundred.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of King Jehoiachin of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, King Evil-merodach of Babylon, in the year he began to reign, showed favor to King Jehoiachin of Judah and brought him out of prison;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth of the month, that Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the year he began to reign, lifted up, the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah, and brought him forth out of prison;
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, in the five and twentieth day of the month, that Evil–merodach king of Babylon in the first year of his reign lifted up the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah, and brought him forth out of prison,
text.NABRE: In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin, king of Judah, on the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month, Evil-merodach, king of Babylon, in the inaugural year of his reign, raised up Jehoiachin, king of Judah, and released him from prison.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the transmigration of Joachin the king of Juda, the twelfth month, the five and twentieth of the month, Evilmerodach the king of Babylon lifted up in the very year of his reign, the head of Joachin the king of Juda, and he brought him out of the prison house.

text.NRSV: he spoke kindly to him, and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
text.Rotherham: and spake with him comfortable words,—and set his throne above the throne of the kings who were with him in Babylon;
text.KJV: And spake kindly unto him, and set his throne above the throne of the kings that were with him in Babylon,
text.NABRE: He spoke kindly to him and gave him a throne higher than the thrones of the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
text.DRB: And he spake with him good things, & he set his throne above the thrones of the kings, that were after himself in Babylon.

text.NRSV: So Jehoiachin put aside his prison clothes, and every day of his life he dined regularly at the king’s table.
text.Rotherham: so he changed his prison garments,—and used to eat bread before his face continually, all the days of his life.
text.KJV: And changed his prison garments: and he did continually eat bread before him all the days of his life.
text.NABRE: Jehoiachin took off his prison garb and ate at the king’s table as long as he lived.
text.DRB: And he changed his prison garments, and he did eat bread before him always all the days of his life.

text.NRSV: For his allowance, a regular allowance was given him by the king of Babylon, a portion every day until the day of his death, as long as he lived.
text.Rotherham: And his allowance, was a continual allowance given him from the king of Babylon the portion of the day upon its own day, until the day of his death,—all the days of his life.
text.KJV: And for his diet, there was a continual diet given him of the king of Babylon, every day a portion until the day of his death, all the days of his life.
text.NABRE: The allowance given him by the king of Babylon was a perpetual allowance, in fixed daily amounts, all the days of his life until the day of his death.
text.DRB: And his allowance of meat, a continual provision of meat was given him by the king of Babylon, every day a certain, even unto the day of his death, all the days of his life.

9.26: Egypt and Judah, Edom and the Ammonites, Moab, and those who live in the wilderness and shave their temples. For all the nations are uncircumcised, even the whole house of Israel is uncircumcised at heart.
